item.title: RVM 在 shell 脚本中不起作用，所以我必须修复它 | RVM did not work in the shell script, so I had to fix it
item.description:
所以前几天我正在构建我的投资组合网站，我决定使用Jekyll 。

我租用的服务器可以通过 RVM 运行 Ruby，因此借助符号链接的强大功能，我可以在服务器上拉取源代码，在那里构建静态站点（可以轻而易举地完成）并安全地将其提供给互联网。

但是我遇到了一个问题 - 简单的脚本不起作用：

 #/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rvm use 2.7.2
 RVM is not a function, selecting rubies with 'rvm use ...' will not work. You need to change your terminal emulator preferences to allow login shell. Sometimes it is required to use `/bin/bash --login` as the command. Please visit https://rvm.io/integration/gnome-terminal/ for an example.

有趣的是，我在终端提示符下使用的 RVM不是/usr/local/rvm/bin/rvm而是作为配置文件的一部分注入的shell 函数。

 # Run type command directly from the shell prompt $ type rvm rvm is a function (function gets printed out) # shell script that contains `type rvm` $ ./test.sh rvm is /usr/local/rvm/bin/rvm

您需要自己获取配置文件

事实证明，您必须获取 RVM source的文件。 但这并没有奏效

显然，RVM 依赖于未绑定的变量，因此使用set -eu运行脚本会使它崩溃：

 #!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu source "/usr/local/rvm/scripts/rvm" rvm use 2.7.2
 /usr/local/rvm/scripts/functions/support: Line 182: _system_name: Unbound variable

因此，我们需要在运行所有 RVM 命令时临时允许此类变量。

示例工作脚本

#!/usr/bin/env bash set +u # WTF: Avoid RVM crashing due to unbound variables source "/usr/local/rvm/scripts/rvm" rvm use 2.7.2 set -eu # Restore strict error checking bundle install JEKYLL_ENV = production bundle exec jekyll build

随意的想法

我记得在 Jenkins 触发的 shell 脚本中尝试使用pyenv时遇到了类似的问题……这可能是一个类似性质的问题。



原文:

So I was building my portfolio site the other day and I decided to use Jekyll.

The server I'm renting can run Ruby via RVM, so with the power of symbolic links, I can pull the source on the server, build the static site there (which can be done in a breeze,) and safely serve it to the Internet.

But I ran into a problem - the script simple as this did not work:

#/usr/bin/env bash set -eu

rvm use 2.7.2

RVM is not a function, selecting rubies with 'rvm use ...' will not work.

You need to change your terminal emulator preferences to allow login shell.
Sometimes it is required to use `/bin/bash --login` as the command.
Please visit https://rvm.io/integration/gnome-terminal/ for an example.

Interesting, so RVM I use at the terminal prompt is not /usr/local/rvm/bin/rvm but a shell function that gets injected as a part of the profile.

# Run type command directly from the shell prompt
$ type rvm
rvm is a function
(function gets printed out)

# shell script that contains `type rvm`
$ ./test.sh
rvm is /usr/local/rvm/bin/rvm

(NGL, I was fairly confused when they said "RVM is not a function..." Like, is RVM complaining that I tried to use RVM as a function when it cannot possibly be a function? Nope! What it means is that there is a shell function version of RVM and rvm must be a function to select Ruby interpreters.)

You need to source the profile yourselves

It turns out you must source the file provided by RVM. There can be two places that RVM may exist:

source "$HOME/.rvm/scripts/rvm" # $HOME installation
source "/usr/local/rvm/scripts/rvm" # System installation

But that did not quite do the trick

Apparently, RVM relies on unbound variables, so running the script with set -eu crashes it:

#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu source "/usr/local/rvm/scripts/rvm"

rvm use 2.7.2

/usr/local/rvm/scripts/functions/support: Line 182: _system_name: Unbound variable

So we need to temporarily allow such variables while we run all the RVM commands.

Example working script 

#!/usr/bin/env bash set +u # WTF: Avoid RVM crashing due to unbound variables source "/usr/local/rvm/scripts/rvm"
rvm use 2.7.2 set -eu # Restore strict error checking bundle install
JEKYLL_ENV=production bundle exec jekyll build

Random thought

I remember having similar issue trying to use pyenv in a Jenkins-triggered shell script... It could've been an issue of the similar nature.
item.author: clpsplug

item.title: 存储中指针和引用的区别 | difference of pointer and reference in storage
item.description:
发表于指针在变量的内存中注册地址。 因此，指针值就是地址。

引用是包含目标变量地址的变量。 可以对目标变量应用超过 1 个引用； 它们可以指向目标变量的相同地址。

在我看来，他们的区别更多是在实践的角度。 对于目标变量，如果你想操作它，它只为你提供一个指针； 而参考，可以有很多，同时节省多余的内存消耗。



原文:

shuyueW1991

Posted on May 10

A pointer registers the address in memory of a variable. Therefore, the pointer value is the address.

A reference is a variable that contains the address of the targeted variable. More than 1 reference can be applied to a targeted variable; they can be pointed to the same address of the targeted variable.

In my opinion, their difference is more on the perspective of practice. For the targeted variable, if you want to manipulate it, it just offers you ONE pointer; whereas, the reference, there can be many, whilst saving redundant memory consumption.
item.author: shuyuew1991

item.title: 通过 useModal 钩子在 React 中使用模态对话框。 尽可能简单。 | Using modal dialogs in React via useModal hook. As simple as possible.
item.description:
通过模态对话框与用户交互的最简单方法是使用浏览器 API，如window.alert 、 window.confirm 、 window.prompt 。 只有一个问题。 您无法自定义它们的外观。

 <body> <div> <button class= "trigger" > I want to drink something! </button> <div class= "result" ></div> </div> <script> const resultElement = document . querySelector ( " .result " ); const setDrink = ( drink ) => { if ( drink ) { resultElement . innerText = `Enjoy your drink, ${ drink } ` ; } else { resultElement . innerText = `` ; } }; document . querySelector ( " .trigger " ). addEventListener ( " click " , () => { setDrink ( null ); const coffee = window . confirm ( " Do you like coffee? " ); if ( coffee ) return setDrink ( " Coffee " ); const tea = window . confirm ( " Do you like tea? " ); if ( tea ) return setDrink ( " Tea " ); setDrink ( " Tequila " ); }); </script> </body>

让我们想象我们以（几乎）相同的方式来做，但是使用React ：

 const OrderDrink = () => { const [ drink , setDrink ] = useState (); const modal = useModal (); return ( < div > < button onClick = { async () => { setDrink ( null ); const coffee = await modal . open ( " Do you like coffee? " ); if ( coffee ) return setDrink ( " Coffee " ); const tea = await modal . open ( " Do you like tea? " ); if ( tea ) return setDrink ( " Tea " ); return setDrink ( " Tequila " ); } } > I want to drink something! </ button > < div > { drink && ` ${ drink } is your drink!` } </ div > < ConfirmModal { ... modal } /> </ div > ); }

看起来不错，不是吗。 魔术发生在useModal钩子内部。 只需在open函数中创建Promise 。 并使用用户交互的结果（有效负载）在close函数中解决它。

 const useModal = ( initial = {}) => { const state = useMemo (() => ({ resolve : null , data : initial . data }), []); const [ opened , setOpened ] = useState ( initial . opened ); const open = useCallback (( data ) => { setOpened ( true ); state . data = data ; return new Promise (( resolve ) => { state . resolve = resolve ; }); }, []); const close = useCallback (( payload ) => { setOpened ( false ); state . resolve ( payload ); }, []); return { opened , data : state . data , close , open }; };

还有一步 - 模态对话框组件：

 const ConfirmModal = ( props ) => { if ( ! props . opened ) { return null ; } return ( < aside > < div > { props . data } < br /> < button onClick = { () => props . close ( true ) } > yes </ button > < button onClick = { () => props . close ( false ) } > no </ button > </ div > </ aside > ); };

您可以创建一组一些可重用的对话框： AlertModal 、 ConfirmModal 、 PromptModal 。 根据具体的用例，它可能是一个带有特定领域逻辑的对话框，如EditProductModal 。



原文:

The easiest way to interact with the user through modal dialogs is to use browser APIs like window.alert, window.confirm, window.prompt. There is only one problem. You cannot customize their appearance.

<body> <div> <button class="trigger">I want to drink something!</button> <div class="result"></div> </div> <script> const resultElement = document.querySelector(".result"); const setDrink = (drink) => { if (drink) { resultElement.innerText = `Enjoy your drink, ${drink}`; } else { resultElement.innerText = ``; } }; document.querySelector(".trigger").addEventListener("click", () => { setDrink(null); const coffee = window.confirm("Do you like coffee?"); if (coffee) return setDrink("Coffee"); const tea = window.confirm("Do you like tea?"); if (tea) return setDrink("Tea"); setDrink("Tequila"); }); </script>
</body>

Let's imagine we do it in (almost) the same way, but using React:

const OrderDrink = () => { const [drink, setDrink] = useState(); const modal = useModal(); return ( <div> <button onClick={async () => { setDrink(null); const coffee = await modal.open("Do you like coffee?"); if (coffee) return setDrink("Coffee"); const tea = await modal.open("Do you like tea?"); if (tea) return setDrink("Tea"); return setDrink("Tequila"); }} > I want to drink something! </button> <div>{drink && `${drink} is your drink!`}</div> <ConfirmModal {...modal} /> </div> );
}

Looks good, doesn't it. The magic happens inside the useModal hook. Just create Promise inside the open function. And resolve it inside the close function with the result (payload) of the user interaction.

const useModal = (initial = {}) => { const state = useMemo(() => ({ resolve: null, data: initial.data }), []); const [opened, setOpened] = useState(initial.opened); const open = useCallback((data) => { setOpened(true); state.data = data; return new Promise((resolve) => { state.resolve = resolve; }); }, []); const close = useCallback((payload) => { setOpened(false); state.resolve(payload); }, []); return { opened, data: state.data, close, open };
};

And one more step - the modal dialog component:

const ConfirmModal = (props) => { if (!props.opened) { return null; } return ( <aside> <div> {props.data} <br /> <button onClick={() => props.close(true)}>yes</button> <button onClick={() => props.close(false)}>no</button> </div> </aside> );
};

You can create a set of some reusable dialogs: AlertModal, ConfirmModal, PromptModal. And depending on specific use cases, it could be a dialog with domain specific logic like EditProductModal.
item.author: devterminal

item.title: 制作 zsh 脚本 | Making a zsh script
item.description:
背景：我每天都在做关于我的学习的小笔记，通常是我学到的任何新东西。 我对此做了一个小记录。
我想用日期和时间命名这个笔记（这是为了方便查找、回忆等），但我还需要以这种格式20210510002211获取日期

现在，终端将使用以下命令轻松输出此日期格式：

给它起一个花哨的名字并快速运行
你需要什么：

保存文件，给它一个花哨的名字（日期）

 chmod 755 datum //datum is the fancy name I'm calling the script

把它放在我自己的路上

  • 运行echo PATH

    • 现在您将拥有此路径./bin/dater

    1. 我们需要在 .zshsrc 文件中添加新路径到 PATH 定义
    2. 做nano ~/.zshsrc
      • 您现在可以编辑该文件，将脚本的路径添加到 PATH 行

    3. 保存并退出

    4. 现在，我们需要刷新 zsh 文件

    5. 做

      source .zshsrc


    现在，只需在终端中输入 dater 就可以输出很长的日期和时间



    原文:

    Background: I have been making small notes about my learnings daily, typically any new thing I learnt. I make a small note about it.
    I want to name this note with dates and time(This is for easy find, recollection etc) but I need to also get the date in this format 20210510002211

    Now, the terminal will readily spew out this date formate with this command:

    ** - try it.***

    -- Now I can't keep that in the terminal, hence why i need to make it into a script. give it a fancy name and run quick
    What do you need:

    -A text editor(I use nano here)

    !/bin/bash
# A script that spews out current long date and time
date  +%Y%m%d%H%M%S
    • save the file giving it a fancy name (dater)
    chmod 755 datum       //datum is the fancy name  I'm calling the script

    --- The script can run now, try

    ./dater


    1. Putting it in my own path
    2. Run echo PATH

    -- It should reveal a the current executable path on the system -- It is good practice to have a special folder for your scripts /bin, create that folder on your home directory.

    Now you will have this path ./bin/dater

    1. We need to add the new path to PATH definition on the .zshsrc file
    2. do nano ~/.zshsrc
      • You will be able to edit that file now, add the path to the script to the PATH line

    3. save and exit

    4. Now, we need to refresh the zsh files

    5. do

      source .zshsrc


    Now, just typing dater in the terminal should spit out that long date and time
item.author: habdams

item.title: HP USB-C Dock G5 未检测到显示器 HDMI 或 DisplayPort - 已解决 | HP USB-C Dock G5 doesn't detecting monitors HDMI or DisplayPort - Resolved
item.description:
凯末尔 C.

发表于我使用 USB-C Dock G5 快一年了； 我已使用显示端口和 HDMI 将 Dock 与两台显示器连接； 在我安装了最后一个 Windows 11 更新并重新启动 PC 后，我的显示器出现了问题... Windows 11 无法识别通过 Dock 连接的外部显示器，但可以识别我是否将显示器直接连接到我的 PC 使用一个HDMI端口！

更新所有驱动程序

  • 重新启动、拔掉电源并重新启动所有这些东西

  • 卸载然后重新安装最新的 Windows 更新

  • 更新最新的 HP BIOS

  • 更新最新的 HP 显卡更新

  • 连接并尝试新电缆

  • 连接并试用新的 USB-C Dock G5

    • 沮丧情绪高涨，但工程师永远不会放弃:)

    最后，我通过以下步骤解决了问题......

    关闭你的笔记本电脑

  • 断开扩展坞

  • 按住电源按钮和 F6 20 秒重置笔记本电脑

  • 启动你的电脑

  • 重新插入 G5 扩展坞

    • 解决！

    我们是无服务器的！ - - May 9 5月9日



    原文:

    Kemal C.

    Posted on May 10

    I use the USB-C Dock G5 for almost one year; I have connected the Dock with two monitors using display ports and HDMI; After I've installed the last windows 11 updates and restart the PC I faced a problem with my monitors... Windows 11 doesn't recognize external monitors, connected over the Dock, but recognize if I connect the monitor directly to my PC using an HDMI port!

    *What doesn't work: *

    • Update all drivers

    • Restarting, unplugging, and restarting all that stuff

    • Uninstalling and then reinstalling the latest windows updates

    • Updating the latest HP BIOS

    • Updating the latest HP Graphics update

    • Connect and try new cables

    • Connect and try the new USB-C Dock G5

    Frustration goes high, but an engineer never gives up :)

    Finally, I fixed the problem with the following steps...

    *WHAT WORKS: S O L U T I O N *

    1. Shut down your laptop

    2. Disconnect the dock

    3. Reset the laptop by holding the power button & F6 for 20 seconds

    4. Start your computer up

    5. Plug the G5 dock back in

    RESOLVED!

    p.s. HP guys should be working harder on this case!

    We're Serverless! - May 9

item.title: Vue 2 Spa 路由，后退按钮 | Vue 2 Spa Routing, Back buttons
item.description:
Disclaimer: Using `Vue 2` with `@vue/composition-api`

曾经徘徊过如何制作自定义后退按钮？

这可能看起来微不足道，对吧？

但是如果你想让它更像一个面包屑，返回按钮从详细视图返回到列表页面怎么办？

看起来很简单：

 router . push ({ name : ' SomeList ' })

好的，但是如果该列表有查询/搜索/分页怎么办？

那么为此，您将需要开始在一个地方或另一个地方跟踪一些信息。

但是，如果您不使用 Vuex，或者您尝试尽可能避免依赖，那么我遇到了一个简单的解决方案。

解决方案：

将您的查询同步到当前路线

首先我们需要将查询参数同步到 vue 路由器：

 import { removeNullProperties } from ' ./useUtilities.js ' ; import { useRouter } from ' ./useRouter.js ' ; // composable function export const syncQuery = ( query ) => { // injected vue router instance const router = useRouter (); const updateQueryParams = () => { const url = new URL ( window . location ); // reseting the search as window.location includes the current query params as well url . search = '' ; // removing any null/empty properties so that the url stays a clean as possible Object . keys ( removeNullProperties ( query )). forEach (( key ) => { url . searchParams . set ( key , query [ key ]); }); const { pathname , search } = url ; const newPath = ` ${ pathname }${ search } ` ; const currentPath = router . currentRoute . fullPath ; // if the path changed let's update vue router if ( newPath !== currentPath ) { router . push ( newPath ); } }; // watched to watch query for changes watch ( () => query , () => { updateQueryParams (); }, { deep : true } ); };

使用 vue router 全局解析保护

现在我们需要存储一些元信息，使用 vue router 时有两个简单的选项Global resolve guards beforeResolve或beforeEach

在这个例子中，我将使用beforeResolve

 router . beforeResolve = ( to , from , next ) => { if ( ! to . meta ) { to . meta = {}; } to . meta . from = { name : from . name , params : from . params , query : from . query , }; }

这增加了我们导航回 from 路线所需的一切。

一个可组合的，让你回到真正的道路

接下来，我编写了另一个可组合的函数，用于自定义后退按钮：

 import { useRouter } from ' ./useRouter.js ' ; export const useRouteBack = ( route ) => { const router = useRouter (); const from = router . currentRoute . meta ?. from ; if ( from && from . name === route . name ) { return from ; } return route ; };

您可以像这样简单地使用它：

 <router-link :to= "useRouteBack({name: 'SomeList'})> back to list </router-link>

就是这样。 我花了相当长的时间收集所有的零碎东西，但这个解决方案似乎是涉及最少工作量/代码的解决方案。

从查询初始化列表页

这实际上取决于您的解决方案，但这是使事情顺利进行的最重要部分。

本质上，您需要确保您的列表页面可以在使用router.query参数时进行初始化。

我所做的是将路由器查询处理添加到列表页面，以便我对 api 的第一次调用匹配从 url 提供的初始参数。

这是每个页面的非常定制的解决方案。 因为您可以拥有一个包含复杂过滤器的页面，该过滤器使用对象对您的查询进行 v 建模，而您的 url/api 只需要简单的数据结构，例如



原文: 
Disclaimer: 
Using `Vue 2` with `@vue/composition-api`

Ever wandered how to make a custom back button?

It might seem trivial, right?

But what if you want to act it more like a breadcrumb, where the back button goes from the details view back to the listing page?

Seems simple enough:

router.push({name:'SomeList'})

Ok but what if that list has query/search/pagination?

Well for that you will need to start tracking some information in one place or other.

There is a plugin for vue:
Vuex Router Sync

But if you're not using Vuex or you try to avoid depencies where possible, there's a simple solution that i've come accross.

The solution:

Sync your query to the current route

first we need to sync query params to vue router:

import { removeNullProperties } from './useUtilities.js';
import { useRouter } from './useRouter.js'; // composable function
export const syncQuery = (query) => { // injected vue router instance const router = useRouter(); const updateQueryParams = () => { const url = new URL(window.location); // reseting the search as window.location includes the current query params as well url.search = ''; // removing any null/empty properties so that the url stays a clean as possible Object.keys(removeNullProperties(query)).forEach((key) => { url.searchParams.set(key, query[key]); }); const { pathname, search } = url; const newPath = `${pathname}${search}`; const currentPath = router.currentRoute.fullPath; // if the path changed let's update vue router if (newPath !== currentPath) { router.push(newPath); } }; // watched to watch query for changes watch( () => query, () => { updateQueryParams(); }, { deep: true } );
};

Use vue router global resolve guard

Now we need to store some meta information, you have two simple options when using vue router Global resolve guards beforeResolve or beforeEach

in this example i'll use beforeResolve

router.beforeResolve = (to, from, next) => { if (!to.meta) { to.meta = {}; } to.meta.from = { name: from.name, params: from.params, query: from.query, };
}

This adds all we need to navigate back to the from route.

A composable to get you a true path back

Next i've wrote another composable function to use on the custom back button:

import { useRouter } from './useRouter.js'; export const useRouteBack = (route) => { const router = useRouter(); const from = router.currentRoute.meta?.from; if (from && from.name === route.name) { return from; } return route;
};

which you can use simply like so:

<router-link :to="useRouteBack({name: 'SomeList'})>
back to list
</router-link>

That's it. I've spent quite some time gathering all the bits and bobs, but this solution seemed like the one involving the least amount of effort/code.

Initializing list page from the query

This will really depend on your solution, but this is the most important part to make things work.

In essence you need to make sure your list page can initialize while using the router.query params.

What i've done is add router query handling to the List pages so that my first calls to the api match the initial params provided from the url.

This is a very custom solution for each page. Since you could have a page with complex filters which v-model your query with Objects and your url/api only needs the simple data structures like
item.author: neophen

item.title: PwnKit (CVE-2021-4034)：Linux 系统服务错误 | PwnKit (CVE-2021-4034): Linux system service bug
item.description:
据称它会影响 Ubuntu、Fedora 和 CentOS 等主要发行版。

本地攻击者可以利用此漏洞将其权限升级为 root 访问权限，并根据其意图执行恶意行为。

影响

在发布该漏洞的技术细节不到三个小时后，该漏洞的利用也已在公共空间中公开，这使得该漏洞更具灾难性。

而且由于它影响了大多数 Linux 发行版，除非修补，否则该漏洞仍然可以被任何规模的黑客利用。

A detailed blog on the same had been published in Beagle Security official website which is available in Beagle Security官方网站上已发布了有关该内容的详细博客，可在

https://beaglesecurity.com/blog/vulnerability/pwnkit-linux-system-bug-allowing-distro-root-access.html https://beaglesecurity.com/blog/vulnerability/pwnkit-linux-system-bug-allowing-distro-root-access.html



原文:

A local privilege escalation vulnerability (named PwnKit CVE-2021-4034) which affects pkexec of PolKit (formerly PolicyKit), a SUID-root program which is installed by default in the majority of Linux distributions was detected by Qualys on 25-January-2022. And it was claimed to affect major distributions such as Ubuntu, Fedora, and CentOS.

This vulnerability can be leveraged by a local attacker to escalate its privileges to root access and perform malicious acts according to his/her intention.

Impact

In less than three hours of publishing the technical details of the vulnerability, the exploit to the same had also been available in the public space which made the vulnerability much more catastrophic.

And since it affects the majority of Linux distributions, unless patched, the vulnerability can still be exploited by hackers of any scale.

A detailed blog on the same had been published in Beagle Security official website which is available in

https://beaglesecurity.com/blog/vulnerability/pwnkit-linux-system-bug-allowing-distro-root-access.html
item.author: deeprajr

item.title: 医疗计费解决方案加快了供应商的收入周期 | Medical Billing Solutions Boost Revenue Cycles for Providers
item.description:
美国医疗保健的复杂性已不是什么秘密。 这是它如此昂贵的部分原因，也是为什么创新的软件解决方案可以帮助解决这个问题的部分原因。 在大多数国家，患者走进医院进行咨询，医生对其进行检查，开具检查和药物处方，患者支付费用，然后回家。 如果它是一个全民医疗保健的国家，那么政府经营的医院不收取任何费用。

但在美利坚合众国，事情就没有这么简单了。 当一个人安排预约时，他的保险被核实，医生的服务被记录下来，然后被编码，最后，索赔被填写并提交给付款人。 付款人将核实一切并报销医生。 这个过程容易出错和效率低下，影响提供者和患者。

 让我们看看计费解决方案如何提供帮助 -

与医疗应用程序集成

在快速推进数字化的时代，数字化的流程数量越多，工作流程就越好。 此外，如果这些解决方案能够相互协作以整合运营，它们将极大地提高医院的生产力。 鉴于此，与 EMR、调度解决方案和远程医疗应用程序集成的医疗计费系统可帮助临床医生极大地加速医疗操作。

与其他临床应用程序集成的医疗计费系统软件将使提供者及其员工能够记录护理、对其进行编码、填写索赔、检查并将其提交给付款人。 在没有数字解决方案的情况下，所有这些活动都是零散的，并逐步进行。 但集成的计费解决方案只需点击几下即可帮助完成所有这些工作，使其更快且无错误。

收入周期管理

据估计，索赔错误每年给医疗保健行业造成数十亿美元的损失。 如前所述，提交索赔的整个过程非常繁琐。 医院越大，患者数量越大，因此需要提交的索赔就越多。 不用说，由于错误而被拒绝或拒绝的索赔是可以预料的。 但是患者计费软件将提交索赔的整个工作流程中涉及的大部分活动数字化和自动化。

因此，被拒绝和被拒绝的索赔数量将大幅减少，从而提高收入。 此外，集成的医疗计费解决方案还减少了人员配备需求以及物理文档的数量。 这些因素在减少管理费用、进一步提高收入方面大有帮助。

此外，小型诊所甚至私人诊所都可以使用可靠的计费解决方案以更高的效率运营。 避免被拒绝的索赔和减少的管理费用有助于单一医生的做法获得更大的收入。 更好的是，他们可以将更多的时间、精力和资源集中在患者护理上。 因此，计费解决方案对提供者和患者来说都是双赢的。

当医疗计费系统的所有好处叠加时，它们有助于降低护理成本，这已成为美国医疗保健的一个主要问题。 随着医疗技术领域越来越多地采用创新解决方案，医疗计费解决方案有很大的发展空间和吸引最优秀人才的空间。



原文:

The complexity of American healthcare is no secret. It’s part of the reason why it’s as expensive as it is, and why innovative software solutions could help with that problem. In most countries, a patient walks into a hospital for a consultation, the doctor examines him, prescribes tests and medication, the person pays for it, and goes home. If it’s a country with universal healthcare, then government-run hospitals don’t charge any money.

But in the United States of America, it is not so simple. When a person schedules an appointment, his insurance is verified, the doctor’s services are documented, they are then coded, and finally, claims are filled out and submitted to payers. The payers would verify everything and reimburse the doctor. This process is prone to errors and inefficiencies, affecting both the providers as well as the patients.

Medical billing solutions go a long way in easing this process and minimizing the chances of human errors. Let’s have a look at how billing solutions help -

Integration with Medical Applications

At a time of rapidly advancing digitization, the greater the number of processes digitized, the better the workflow. Furthermore, if these solutions can work with each other to consolidate operations, they’d immensely bolster productivity at hospitals. In light of this, medical billing systems that integrate with EMRs, scheduling solutions, and telehealth applications help clinicians accelerate medical operations immensely.

A medical billing system software that’s integrated with other clinical applications would enable providers and their staff to document the care, code it, fill out the claims, check it, and submit it to payers. In the absence of a digital solution, all of these activities are fragmented and proceed step-by-step. But an integrated billing solution can help complete all of this with a few clicks, making it faster and free of errors.

Revenue Cycle Management

It is estimated that errors in claims cost billions to the healthcare industry every year. The entire process of submitting claims is highly tedious, as mentioned earlier. The bigger the hospital, greater the volume of patients, and so, more the claims that need to be submitted. Needless to say, claims rejected or denied due to mistakes are to be expected. But a patient billing software digitizes and automates most of the activities involved in the entire workflow of submitting claims.

As a result, the number of rejected and denied claims would reduce drastically, improving revenues. Furthermore, integrated medical billing solutions also reduce the staffing requirements, as well as the amount of physical documentation. These factors go a long way in reducing overhead, further improving revenues.

Additionally, smaller clinics and even private practices could operate with greater efficiency using a reliable billing solution. Avoiding denied claims and reduced overhead help single-physician practices earn greater revenues. Better yet, they can focus more time, energy, and resources on patient care. So, Billing solutions are a win-win for providers as well as patients.

When all the benefits of medical billing systems are stacked up, they help reduce the cost of care, which has grown to become a major problem with healthcare in the United States. With the increasing adoption of innovative solutions in medical technology, there is plenty of room for medical billing solutions to grow and attract the best talent.
