item.title: 静态类型与动态类型：差异解释 | Static Typing VS Dynamic Typing: The Difference Explained
item.description:
阿琼女士

发表于您可能已经听说过在讨论编程语言时出现的术语“静态类型”和“动态类型”。 但它们是什么意思，为什么知道它很重要？

要了解静态类型和动态类型之间的区别，我们先来谈谈类型检查。 类型检查是编译器或解释器使用的一个过程，以确保以每种语言的正确语法编写程序。 静态类型检查将在您使用编译器编写代码时识别任何错误，而动态类型检查将在运行时识别任何错误。

什么是静态打字？

静态类型是许多编程语言中使用的一种约定，用于定义代码中变量的类型。 变量的类型是在编译时确定的（程序代码转换为机器代码的时间段）。

静态类型对开发人员来说可能是有益的，因为它可以让他们更容易地理解他们正在编写的代码，并且可以帮助他们防止错误。 静态类型编程语言的示例包括 C、Java、C++、Scala 等……

简单来说，静态类型意味着您在程序启动之前告诉计算机程序中的数据类型。

静态类型的优点

类型安全有助于防止意外的类型转换（例如，数字类型之间），这可能会产生不正确的结果或导致运行时错误。 静态类型使新开发人员更容易理解和修改现有代码，因为可以通过分析源代码本身轻松确定每个变量的预期类型。

它可以帮助在开发周期的早期检测错误。 编译器或解释器在运行代码之前检查类型错误。 这意味着您可以在编译时发现错误，而不是在运行时代码崩溃时感到惊讶。

什么是动态类型？

动态类型是一种在许多编程语言和工具中使用的技术，是一种确定在运行时（即代码运行时）存储的数据类型的方法。 与静态类型不同，其中所有变量都提前键入并在整个程序执行过程中保持不变，动态类型允许变量的数据类型在执行期间更改。 这很有用，因为它允许程序员在同一个变量中存储不同类型的数据； 但是，它也可能导致代码中出现意外错误。

简而言之，动态类型是一种类型，您不必在创建变量时决定变量的类型。

动态类型编程语言的示例包括 JavaScript、Python、PHP、Ruby 等……

动态类型的优点

更容易写。 声明变量类型可能很痛苦，尤其是在需要多次声明相同类型的多行语句中。 更容易重构代码。 重命名动态类型的变量不需要更改它们的声明或类型注释； 使用静态类型，您必须更改代码库中变量类型的所有引用。

更快的开发时间。 无需声明变量类型，您可以简单地开始编码，然后运行您的代码。

结论

不可否认，静态类型非常强大，它能够避免运行时错误。 动态类型更容易集成，并且不需要对现有代码进行大量更改。 哪一个赢了？ 这取决于您希望如何处理您的项目。 明智地选择——你今天选择的类型系统会在未来几年影响你的代码。

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— 雅库布·安德杰耶夫斯基—— 阿卜杜拉·帕拉查—— Apr 27 4月27日



原文:

Arjun M S

Posted on May 10

You’ve probably heard the terms “static typing” and “dynamic typing” thrown around in discussions of programming languages. But what do they mean, and why is it important to know?

To understand the difference between static and dynamic typing, let’s first talk about type checking. Type checking is a process used by compilers or interpreters to ensure that a program is written with correct syntax for each language. Static type checking will identify any errors when you’re writing code using a compiler, whereas dynamic type checking will identify any errors at runtime.

What is Static Typing?

Static typing is a convention used in many programming languages that defines the type of variable in the code. The type of a variable is determined at compile time(the period when the programming code is converted to the machine code).

Static typing can be beneficial for developers because it allows them to more easily understand the code they are writing and it can help them to prevent errors. Examples of statically typed programming languages include C, Java, C++,Scala, etc…

In simple words, Static typing means you tell the computer what type of data is in the program before the program starts.

Example in Java:

String x = “Hello world!”; // x is a string
int y = 15;  // y is an integer

Advantages of Static Typing

Type safety can help prevent unintended type conversions (for example, between numeric types), which can produce incorrect results or cause runtime errors. Static typing makes it easier for a new developer to understand and modify existing code, as the expected types of each variable can easily be determined through analysis of the source code itself.

It can help detect bugs earlier in the development cycle. The compiler or interpreter checks for type errors before running your code. This means you can find errors at compile time instead of being surprised at runtime when your code crashes.

What is Dynamic Typing?

Dynamic typing, a technique used in many programming languages and tools, is a method of determining the type of data being stored at runtime (i.e., when code is running). In contrast to static typing, where all variables are typed ahead of time and remain constant throughout a program’s execution, dynamic typing allows the data type of a variable to change during execution. This can be useful because it allows programmers to store different types of data in the same variable; however, it can also lead to unexpected errors in code.

In simple words, Dynamic typing is a type of typing where you don’t have to decide what the type of a variable is when you make the variable.

Examples of dynamically typed programming languages include JavaScript,Python, PHP,Ruby, etc…

Example in Python:

$ name = “John Doe” // $name is now of type string
$ age = 15; // $age is now an integer

Advantages of Dynamic Typing

Easier to write. Declaring variable types can be a pain, especially in multi-line statements when you need to declare the same type multiple times. Easier to refactor code. Renaming variables that are dynamically typed does not require changing their declaration or type annotation; with static typing, you would have to change all references in the codebase for the variable’s type.

Faster development time. Without needing to declare variable types, you can simply start coding and then run your code.

Conclusion

Static typing is undeniably powerful, with its ability to avoid runtime errors. Dynamic typing is easier to integrate, and doesn’t require extensive changes to existing code. Which one wins? It depends on how you want to approach your project. Choose wisely — the type system you choose today can affect your code for years to come.

Cristian Fernando - Apr 27

Jakub Andrzejewski - Apr 27

Abdullah Paracha - Apr 27
item.author: arjunms

item.title: 探索科莫多（草稿） | Exploring Komodor (draft)
item.description:
戴安娜🦪

发表于这是我第一次尝试使用 Kubernetes！

资源：

终端命令安装 Helm，一个允许用户将应用程序和服务安装到 k8s 集群的工具。

$ brew install helm

安装 k8s 命令行工具。
$ brew install kubectl $ kubectl version - client

安装 Civo，一个允许用户快速创建 k8s 集群的云服务。
$ brew tap civo/tools $ brew install civo

命名并保存 API 密钥。
问：这个API key属于哪个集群？ 我在 Civo Web UI 上创建了一个名为“demo-cluster”的用户界面。 这个集群与其余的有什么关系？ 后来我在终端中创建了一个名为“cool-hail”的集群。
$ civo apikey save myapikey 1BUQ5KFUR5eE5skRqlIsh3JmjneCWRsdKsI9DB15lNCNMYJ5Pj $ civo apikey current myapikey $ civo apikey ls $ civo apikey show $ civo

在终端中创建一个集群。
$ civo kubernetes create
 创建了集群“cool-hail”。

$ kubectl get nodes


与服务器 localhost:8080 的连接被拒绝 - 您是否指定了正确的主机或端口？

$ helm repo add komodorio https://helm-charts.komodor.io; helm repo update; helm upgrade - install k8s-watcher komodorio/k8s-watcher - set apiKey=9d0fc2ae-8e74–417c-970f-5949100d9f3d - set watcher.clusterName=cool-hail - wait - timeout=90s && open https://app.komodor.com/main/services 错误：Kubernetes 集群无法访问：获取“ http://localhost:8080/version”：拨号 tcp [::1]:8080：连接：连接被拒绝

几乎每次我尝试添加集群时都会遇到这个问题。

然而，他们似乎最终得到了整合。

我应该如何进行？

安德鲁·阿什拉夫·萨布里—— 奥斯汀坎宁安 - Jan 12 1月12日



原文:

Diana 🦪

Posted on May 10

Here's my first attempt at using Kubernetes!

Resources:

Terminal Commands Install Helm, a tool that allows users to install applications and services to k8s clusters.

$ brew install helm

Install k8s command line tool.
$ brew install kubectl $ kubectl version - client

Install Civo, a cloud service that allows users to quickly create k8s clusters.
$ brew tap civo/tools $ brew install civo

Name and save the API key.
Question: To which cluster does this API key belong? I have created one on the Civo web UI called 'demo-cluster'. What does this cluster have to do with the rest of this? Later I created a cluster in the terminal called 'cool-hail'.
$ civo apikey save myapikey 1BUQ5KFUR5eE5skRqlIsh3JmjneCWRsdKsI9DB15lNCNMYJ5Pj $ civo apikey current myapikey $ civo apikey ls $ civo apikey show $ civo

Create a cluster in the terminal.
$ civo kubernetes create
 Cluster 'cool-hail' was created.

$ kubectl get nodes


The connection to the server localhost:8080 was refused - did you specify the right host or port?

$ helm repo add komodorio https://helm-charts.komodor.io; helm repo update; helm upgrade - install k8s-watcher komodorio/k8s-watcher - set apiKey=9d0fc2ae-8e74–417c-970f-5949100d9f3d - set watcher.clusterName=cool-hail - wait - timeout=90s && open https://app.komodor.com/main/services "komodorio" has been added to your repositories Hang tight while we grab the latest from your chart repositories… …Successfully got an update from the "komodorio" chart repository Update Complete. ⎈Happy Helming!⎈

Error: Kubernetes cluster unreachable: Get "http://localhost:8080/version": dial tcp [::1]:8080: connect: connection refused

It seems komodor generates new API keys for each cluster I list. When choosing to "Add a Kubernetes cluster" do they actually mean creating a new cluster since a new API key is created each time?

I came across this issue almost every time I attempted to add a cluster.

However, it seems they ended up getting integrated.

How should I proceed?

Nicolas El Khoury - Jan 12

Andrew Ashraf Sabry - Jan 12

Austin Cunningham - Jan 12
item.author: dianasoyster

item.title: 2022 年 8 门最佳反应课程 | 8 Best React Courses 2022
item.description:
随着 Web 开发的发展和发展，我们软件开发人员继续学习和自学新技术非常重要。 今年要添加到您的技能堆栈中的一个很棒的框架（如果您还没有的话）是 React js。

随着公司在其技术堆栈中采用 React，React 开发人员是世界上最受欢迎的开发人员之一。 因此，如果您一直在考虑它，那么现在是您在 2022 年开始学习 React 之旅的最佳时机。这里有一些很棒的课程可供开始！

为了实现这一点，他们为他们的代码截屏视频创建了一种新的视频格式，他们称之为“稀松布”。 在此，您可以在任何给定时间暂停视频并编辑您的讲师用来教您的代码。 这使课程变得有趣和互动，并为学生带来快速学习。

这是由 Epic 讲师 Kent C. Dodds 开设的关于 React 的史诗课程。 本课程教你 React 基础知识、React Hooks、高级 React Hooks、高级 React 模式、React 性能、测试 React 应用程序、React Suspense，以及关于“构建 Epic React 应用程序”的最后一节。

除了这个 19 小时的大型深度课程外，您还可以在注册后获得与行业专家的多次奖金访谈。 这是一门付费课程，不适合新手开发人员，它需要对 HTML、CSS 和现代 JavaScript 有深入的了解。

赫尔辛基大学的Full Stack Open是互联网上最好的全栈 React 开发课程之一。 本课程教您如何使用 JavaScript 构建现代 Web 应用程序，重点是使用 React 构建单页面应用程序，这些应用程序使用由 NodeJS 构建的 REST API。

尽管它是一门全栈开发课程，但它包含大量有关构建 React 应用程序的内容。 该课程每年更新一次，因此每年都会添加更多关于最新网络技术的精彩内容。 截至 2022 年 2 月，本课程包含有关 React、Redux、NodeJS、MongoDB、测试、GraphQL、TypeScript、React Native、CI/CD、容器和关系数据库的部分。

这是一门基于文本的课程，最好的部分是它是 100% 免费的。 如果你是一个想要学习现代全栈开发的人，这就是你的方式。 如果您只想学习 React，请考虑阅读本课程的 React 相关部分，您一定会喜欢的。

这不是一门课程，而是 React 的官方文档，这比直接从源代码学习 React 更好。 该文档易于理解，维护良好，并且充满了示例。 几个月前，React 团队也发布了相同文档的更新beta 版本。 新文档强调功能组件，而旧文档更侧重于类组件。

截至 2022 年 5 月，Beta 文档的Learn React部分和API 参考部分已完成约 70% 和约 20%。 如果您更喜欢阅读而不是观看或喜欢直接跳到文档而不是第三方教程，您应该检查一次。

与 Epic React 不同，它是初学者入门的理想课程。 它带您从绝对基础到使用 Codesandbox、GitHub 和 Netlify 部署您的 React 应用程序。

该课程是 100% 免费的，如果您更喜欢视频讲座而不是博客文章和文档，那么本课程是开始您的 React 之旅的重要一步。

了解概念是一回事，将这些概念应用到项目中是另一回事。 本课程将准确地教给您，如何实施您所学的知识并用它构建很酷的项目。

这是 Freecodecamp 提供的基于项目的YouTube 视频课程，您将在其中使用 React 构建 15 个很酷的项目。 如果您感觉有点痒并想从事更多项目，或者您的首选课程不包括很多项目，您可以参加本课程。

尽量不要陷入教程地狱，因为最终你必须学会​​自己提出解决方案，但一开始，构建项目观看教程完全没问题。

完整的 React 开发人员是您在 2022 年成为一名完整的 React 开发人员的指南。除了教授 React 的核心概念外，本课程还介绍了路由、Redux、Firebase、Stripe 支付、高级 React 钩子、部署、上下文 API、 GraphQL、React 性能、渐进式 Web 应用程序等等。

您将在构建大型电子商务 React 应用程序时学习所有这些概念，本课程还将教您核心 React 概念之外的东西，这些概念对于构建企业级应用程序很重要。

这是 Traversy Media 著名的 Brad Traversy 开设的一门出色的Udemy 课程。 对于那些想要更深入并在学习课程的同时构建更多项目的人来说，这是一门很棒的课程。 本课程共有四个 React 项目，其中还包括一个全栈 MERN 应用程序和一个 Firebase 9 应用程序。

本课程从 React 的基础知识开始，一直到您的项目部署，并包括您在构建 React 应用程序时大部分时间使用的所有常见的 React 概念。

这是一门全面的课程，重点是构建不同的项目，让您近距离了解您将在现实世界中使用 React 做什么。

离别的思念

如果你是刚开始学习 React 的初学者，我给你的建议是首先检查我上面提到的所有课程，浏览一下内容，看看所有课程中哪些主题相似，每个课程有哪些独特之处课程。

再次考虑一下你学习 React 的动机，然后选择你认为可以让你更接近目标的课程。 最终，您将通过经验和构建项目来学习一切，但重要的是在一开始就坚持一条路，以便更快地学习。

如果您对这些课程或您的 React 学习之旅有任何困惑或疑问，请查看我的Discord 技术作家社区并在Twitter 上与我联系。

快乐编码！



原文:

As web development grows and evolves, it's important that we software developers continue to learn and teach ourselves new technologies. One great framework to add to your skill stack this year (if you haven't already) is React js.

React was introduced in 2013 by Facebook, and has since successfully risen above most other frontend frameworks in terms of usage and popularity. React developers are some of the most in-demand developers around the world as companies adopt React in their tech stack. So if you've been thinking about it, now is the perfect time to jump in and start your journey of learning React in 2022. Here are some great courses to start with!

1. Scrimba's React Course

Scrimba is an online learning platform whose "goal is to create the best possible coding school at the lowest possible cost for students."(from Scrimba)

To make it possible, they have created a new video format for their code screencasts which they call "scrims". In this, you can pause the video at any given time and edit the code which your instructor was using to teach you. This makes the course fun and interactive and results in quick learning for the students.

2. Epic React by Kent C Dodds

This is an epic course on React by an Epic Instructor, Kent C. Dodds. Kent is a JavaScript engineer, Co-Founder, and Director of Developer Experience at Remix.

This course teaches you React Fundamentals, React Hooks, Advanced React Hooks, Advanced React Patterns, React Performance, Testing React Apps, React Suspense, and a final section on "Build an Epic React App".

Apart from this massive 19 hours in-depth course, you'll also get multiple bonus interviews with industry experts once you enroll in it. This is a paid course and not the ideal one for novice developers, it requires a prior in-depth understanding of HTML, CSS, and modern JavaScript.

3. Full Stack Open

Full Stack Open by the University of Helsinki is one of the best Full Stack React Development courses on the internet. This course teaches you how to build modern web applications with JavaScript centering around building single page applications with React that use REST APIs built with NodeJS.

Although it's a full-stack development course, it contains a great deal of content on building React apps. The course is updated annually and therefore more great content on the latest web technologies is added every year. As of Feb 2022, this course contains sections on React, Redux, NodeJS, MongoDB, Testing, GraphQL, TypeScript, React Native, CI/CD, Containers, and Relational Databases.

It's a text-based course and the best part is that it's 100% free. If you are someone who wants to badly learn modern full-stack development, this is your way. If you only want to learn React, consider reading the React related parts of the course, you'll certainly love it.

4. React Docs

This is not a course, but official documentation of React, and what more good it can be than learning React directly from the source. The documentation is easy to understand, well-maintained, and filled with examples. An updated beta version of the same documentation has also been released a few months ago by React team. The new documentation emphasizes functional components while the old docs focused more on class components.

As of May 2022, the Learn React section and API reference section of the beta docs are ~70% and ~20% finished. If you prefer reading more than watching or love to jump directly on docs instead of third party tutorials, you should check it out once.

5. Egghead React beginners course

Eggheads React course is also a great option to get your hands dirty. Another course taught by Kent C. Dodds in our list, it has a total timespan of 2 hours 35 minutes and is divided into 30 small sections.

Its an ideal course for beginners to get started, unlike Epic React. It takes you from absolute basics to deploying your React application with Codesandbox, GitHub, and Netlify.

The course is 100% free and if you prefer video lectures over blogposts and documentation, this course is a great step to start your React journey.

6. 15 React projects course by Freecodecamp

Learning about concepts is one thing and implementing those concepts into projects is another. This course will teach you exactly that, how to implement whatever you have learned and build cool projects with it.

This is a project-based YouTube video course by Freecodecamp where youll build 15 cool projects in React. You can take up this course if youre feeling a bit itchy and want to work on more projects or if your preferred courses dont include many projects.

Just try not to fall into a tutorial hell because eventually you have to learn to come up with solutions on your own but in the beginning, its completely fine to build projects watching tutorials.

7. Complete React Developer in 2022 w/ Redux, Hooks, GraphQL)

The Complete React Developer is your guide to being a complete React developer in 2022. Apart from teaching the core concepts of React, this course also takes a look at Routing, Redux, Firebase, Stripe payments, Advanced React hooks, Deployment, Context API, GraphQL, React performance, Progressive Web Apps and so much more.

You will learn all these concepts while building a massive E-commerce React app and this course will also teach you things outside the core React concepts which are important in building enterprise-level applications.

8. React Front to Back 2022

This is an excellent Udemy course by Traversy Media's famed Brad Traversy. Its a great course for those who want more depth and also build more projects alongside working on the course. There are a total of four React projects in this course which also includes a fullstack MERN app and a Firebase 9 app.

This course starts with the basics of React and goes up to the deployment of your projects and includes all the common concepts of React which youll most of the time use while building React applications.

Its a well rounded course with a focus on building different projects giving you a close view of what youll be doing with React in the real world.

Parting Thoughts

If you are a beginner just looking to start learning React, my suggestion to you would be to first check all the courses I mentioned above, glance over the contents, and see what topics are similar across all the courses and what are unique things in each course.

Think about your motivation for learning React once again and choose the course which you think will take you close to your goals. Eventually, youll learn everything with experience and building projects but Its important to stick to one path, in the beginning, to learn things faster.

Check out my Discord community of technical writers and reach out to me on Twitter if youve any confusion or question about these courses or your React learning journey in general.

Happy Coding!

item.title: App Generator - 使用 AppSeed 编写简单的仪表板 | App Generator - Code a simple Dashboard using AppSeed
item.description:
你好编码员！

本文介绍了如何使用AppSeed通过可视化界面生成simple Flask Dashboard 。 用户无需帐户即可访问该服务，根据他们的选择生成一个新项目并从 Github（MIT 许可证）下载代码。

谢谢阅读！

这是解释该过程的 Youtube视频。

任何没有帐户的人都可以使用该生成器，并且可以在 Github 上实时访问源代码。 基于许可许可证，源代码可用于商业项目和电子学习活动。

让我们重申视频中介绍的步骤：

在不到 1 分钟的时间内，源代码应该可以在 Github 上下载。 在本地环境中开始使用可能更简单的方法是使用Docker设置。 要使用该项目，只需在项目的根目录中键入一个命令：

 $ docker-compose up --build

执行命令后，应该可以在浏览器中访问 Datta Able：

Thanks for reading! 如需更多资源，请随时访问：



原文:

Hello coders!

This article explains how to use AppSeed to generate a simple Flask Dashboard using a visual interface. Users can access the service without an account, generate a new project based on their selections and download the code from Github (MIT License).

Thanks for reading!

Here is the Youtube video that explains the process.

✨ How to use the service

The generator can be used by anyone without an account and the sources can be accessed in real-time on Github. Based on the permissive license, source code can be used in commercial projects and eLearning activities.

Let's reiterate the steps presented in the video:

👉 The User selects the design - Datta Able in our DEMO

👉 Select the preferred backend

👉 Configure the product: Database and Deployment

👉 Confirm the action and wait to download the sources:

In less than 1 minute the sources should be available on Github for download. Probably the easier way to get started in a local environment is to use the Docker set up. To use the project, just type a single command in the root of the project:

$ docker-compose up --build

Once the command is executed, Datta Able should be accessible in the browser:

Thanks for reading! For more resources, feel free to access:

item.title: 算法：Tipos de Dados | Algoritmos: Tipos de Dados
item.description:
贴士德达多斯

示例：10.9、57.69、7.1 e 等....

Dados literais: neste tipo de dados, contém sequências de números, letras e símbolos especias(geralmente utilizamos com " aspas duplas dentro de uma variável). Dados literais：neste tipo de dados、contém sequências de números、letras e símbolos especias（geralmente utilizamos com “aspas duplas dentro de uma variável）。

Quando trabalhamos com dados(que no nosso caso é o tempo inteiro), devemos ter cuidado e consciência de como podemos utiliza-lós e trata-lós. Quando trabalhamos com dados（que no nosso caso é o tempo inteiro），devemos ter cuidado e consciência de como podemos utiliza-lós e trata-lós。



原文:

Olá dev tudo bem? eu sou Samuel Balena e faço parte do grupo de embaixadores da DIO Campus Experts, e hoje estou aqui para discutir mais um assunto que é muito importante para a nossa carreira como desenvolvedor. Então vamos lá!

Tipos de Dados

No contexto do mundo da programação, dados é tudo aquilo que pode ser fornecido para o computador, de forma que possam ser processados para resolver um problema ou gerar informações.

Primeiro vamos definir melhor esses termos, então vamos lá.

Dados: é como se fosse um fragmento de um informação, por exemplo um número qualquer pode ser um dado, da mesma forma que uma palavra também pode ser um dado. Mesmo que fora de contexto, ainda não deixam de ser dados.

Informação: por sua vez um informação são dados dentro de um contexto, já que por sua vez um dado sem contexto não faça muito sentido, por exemplo vamos usar uma sequêcia de números: 11 95396-8948. Esses dados podem ser considerados uma informação, que nesse caso seria um número de telefone.

Intrução: simplismente é o que utilizamos para desenvolvimento de sistemas. Podemos dizer que se constitui numa sequência de palavras que o computador possa entender para realizar o processamento de dados.

Tipos de dados

Assim como definimos dados acima, vamos citar os seus tipos que existem.

Númericos inteiros: esses dados consistem em números inteiros e nas operações matemáticas. Por exemplo, nas operações de subtração, adição, divisão e multiplicação.

Númericos Reais: diferente de números inteiros, os números reais por sua vez contém uma parte inteira e outra fracionária. Exemplos: 10.9, 57.69, 7.1 e etc....

Dados literais: neste tipo de dados, contém sequências de números, letras e símbolos especias(geralmente utilizamos com " aspas duplas dentro de uma variável).

Quando trabalhamos com dados(que no nosso caso é o tempo inteiro), devemos ter cuidado e consciência de como podemos utiliza-lós e trata-lós.

item.title: 关于我们第一次生产 hasura 部署的想法 | Thoughts on our first production hasura deployment
item.description:
工作流程归结为：

  1. 定义您的数据库表和关系（使用普通 SQL DDL 语句）
  2. 启动哈苏拉
  3. 配置权限规则
  4. 将其连接到 auth0 之类的东西以进行用户管理
  5. 使用当下最热门的东西来实现一个 ui

我对哈苏拉的第一个想法就是它有多无聊。 一切都……有效吗？ 就像，你发现自己需要为你的应用程序添加一个新功能，并且不费吹灰之力，你就会发现自己已经完成了大量的空闲时间。

您编写一个 graphQL 查询（这是最难的部分），运行 codegen，然后它会为您提供一个可以在组件中使用的钩子。

这是一个使用一堆数据绘制表格的示例，包括分页、排序和按事件名称过滤的搜索字段。 这是我们在生产中使用的：

 const EVENTS_QUERY = gql ` query Events( $limit: Int = 10 $offset: Int = 0 $order_by: [events_order_by!] = [] $where: events_bool_exp = {} ) { events(limit: $limit, offset: $offset, order_by: $order_by, where: $where) { date eventnumber name seriesevent { id seriesid series { seriesname } } } events_aggregate(where: $where) { aggregate { count } } } ` ; export const DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE = 10 ; export const FieldContainsComparison = ( s : string ): String_Comparison_Exp => { return { _ilike : `% ${ s } %` }; }; export function EventListContainer ( props : { searchText : string }) { const [ offset , setOffset ] = useState ( 0 ); const [ orderBy , setOrderBy ] = useState < Events_Order_By > ({ date : Order_By . Desc , }); let filter : Events_Bool_Exp | undefined = undefined ; if ( props . searchText !== "" ) { filter = { name : FieldContainsComparison ( props . searchText ) }; } const { loading , error , data , previousData , refetch } = useEventsQuery ({ variables : { limit : DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE , offset : offset , where : filter , order_by : orderBy , }, }); const latest = data ?? previousData ; if ( error ) return < div > Error : { error . message } < /div> ; /* Don't attempt to draw the table until after the first set of data has been loaded. */ if ( loading && ! latest ) return < Loading loading = { loading } /> ; return ( <> < Loading loading = { loading } / > < table > < thead > < tr > < td > < div > Event Number < OrderByControls setAsc = {() => setOrderBy ({ eventnumber : Order_By . Asc })} setDesc = {() => setOrderBy ({ eventnumber : Order_By . Desc })} / > < /div > < /td > < td > < div > Event Name < OrderByControls setAsc = {() => setOrderBy ({ name : Order_By . Asc })} setDesc = {() => setOrderBy ({ name : Order_By . Desc })} / > < /div > < /td > < td > < div > Date < OrderByControls setAsc = {() => setOrderBy ({ date : Order_By . Asc })} setDesc = {() => setOrderBy ({ date : Order_By . Desc })} / > < /div > < /td > < td > < div > Series < OrderByControls setAsc = {() => setOrderBy ({ seriesevent : { seriesid : Order_By . Asc } }) } setDesc = {() => setOrderBy ({ seriesevent : { seriesid : Order_By . Desc } }) } / > < /div > < /td > < td > Action < /td > < /tr > < /thead > < tbody > { latest ?. events . map (( event ) => ( < tr key = { event . eventnumber } > < td > { event . eventnumber } < /td > < td > { event . name } < /td > < td > { event . date } < /td > < td > { event . seriesevent ?. series ?. seriesname ?? "" } < /td > < td > < Link to = { `/dashboard/events/ ${ event . eventnumber } ` } > < img width = " 20 " height = " 20 " src = " /edit.svg " /> < /Link > < /td > < /tr > ))} < /tbody > < /table > < Pagination pageSize = { DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE } total = { latest ?. events_aggregate . aggregate ?. count } offset = { offset } setOffset = { setOffset } / > < / > ); }

我们使用 Auth0 进行用户管理。 弄清楚如何使用正确的有效负载创建 JWT 绝对不是一件容易的事，但也没有花那么长时间。 让 hasura 接受这些 JWT 非常容易 - 只需将 JWT 密码复制并粘贴到 env 变量中即可。

我们应用程序中的一个屏幕显示来自第三方 REST API 的数据。 我们设置了一个 hasura 操作以将 REST 端点公开为 graphql 查询，并且它像其他所有内容一样弹出在 graphql api 定义中。 漂亮整齐！

那么，有什么可以改进的呢？ 我想说操纵数据的经验需要一些工作。 如果您尝试插入一条记录但出现了不正确的情况，您将遇到约束违规或权限错误。 没有足够的信息来为最终用户构建正确的错误消息。 我认为缺少的另一个关键功能是将字段标记为可选或必需的能力。 现在出现的 graphql 定义将每个字段都作为可选字段，尽管我知道其中许多定义如果省略会导致错误。 我希望考虑到 Hasura 的疯狂筹款，他们能够解决这些问题，但到目前为止，我对这个产品真的很满意。



原文:

Hasura is a tool to generate an api directly from your database. The workflow boils down to:

  1. define your database tables and relationships (using normal SQL DDL statements)
  2. spin up hasura
  3. configure permissions rules
  4. hook it into something like auth0 for user management
  5. implement a ui using whatever's hot at the moment

My first thought about hasura is just how boring it is. Everything just.. works? Like, you find yourself needing to add a new feature to your app and with very little effort you find yourself finished with loads of time to spare.

The UI side is also pretty great - I'm using @graphql-codegen/typescript-react-apollo to generate client code for react. You write a graphQL query (this is the hardest part), run the codegen, then it gives you a hook you can use in your component.

Here's an example that draws a table with a bunch of data, including pagination, ordering and a search field that filters by event names. It's what we're using in production:

const EVENTS_QUERY = gql` query Events( $limit: Int = 10 $offset: Int = 0 $order_by: [events_order_by!] = [] $where: events_bool_exp = {} ) { events(limit: $limit, offset: $offset, order_by: $order_by, where: $where) { date eventnumber name seriesevent { id seriesid series { seriesname } } } events_aggregate(where: $where) { aggregate { count } } }
`; export const DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE = 10; export const FieldContainsComparison = (s: string): String_Comparison_Exp => { return { _ilike: `%${s}%` };
}; export function EventListContainer(props: { searchText: string }) { const [offset, setOffset] = useState(0); const [orderBy, setOrderBy] = useState<Events_Order_By>({ date: Order_By.Desc, }); let filter: Events_Bool_Exp | undefined = undefined; if (props.searchText !== "") { filter = { name: FieldContainsComparison(props.searchText) }; } const { loading, error, data, previousData, refetch } = useEventsQuery({ variables: { limit: DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE, offset: offset, where: filter, order_by: orderBy, }, }); const latest = data ?? previousData; if (error) return <div>Error: {error.message}</div>;
 /* Don't attempt to draw the table until after the first set of data has been loaded. */ if (loading && !latest) return <Loading loading={loading} />;
 return ( <> <Loading loading={loading} />
 <table> <thead> <tr> <td> <div> Event Number <OrderByControls setAsc={() => setOrderBy({ eventnumber: Order_By.Asc })} setDesc={() => setOrderBy({ eventnumber: Order_By.Desc })} />
 </div>
 </td>
 <td> <div> Event Name <OrderByControls setAsc={() => setOrderBy({ name: Order_By.Asc })} setDesc={() => setOrderBy({ name: Order_By.Desc })} />
 </div>
 </td>
 <td> <div> Date <OrderByControls setAsc={() => setOrderBy({ date: Order_By.Asc })} setDesc={() => setOrderBy({ date: Order_By.Desc })} />
 </div>
 </td>
 <td> <div> Series <OrderByControls setAsc={() => setOrderBy({ seriesevent: { seriesid: Order_By.Asc } }) } setDesc={() => setOrderBy({ seriesevent: { seriesid: Order_By.Desc } }) } />
 </div>
 </td>
 <td>Action</td>
 </tr>
 </thead>
 <tbody> {latest?.events.map((event) => ( <tr key={event.eventnumber}> <td>{event.eventnumber}</td>
 <td>{event.name}</td>
 <td>{event.date}</td>
 <td>{event.seriesevent?.series?.seriesname ?? ""}</td>
 <td> <Link to={`/dashboard/events/${event.eventnumber}`}> <img width="20" height="20" src="/edit.svg" /> </Link>
 </td>
 </tr>
 ))} </tbody>
 </table>
 <Pagination pageSize={DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE} total={latest?.events_aggregate.aggregate?.count} offset={offset} setOffset={setOffset} />
 </>
 );
}

We went with Auth0 for user management. Figuring out how to create JWTs with the right payloads was definitely not straight forward, but it didn't take that long either. Getting hasura to accept those JWTs was very easy - just copy and paste the JWT secret into an env variable and you're good to go.

One of the screens in our app displays data from a third party REST API. We set up a hasura action to expose the REST endpoint as a graphql query and it pops up in the graphql api definitions like everything else. Pretty neat!

So, what could be improved? I'd say the experience of manipulating data needs some work. If you try to insert a record and something's not right, you'll either get a constraint violation or a permissions error. There's just not enough information to build a proper error message for end users. Another key feature I think is missing is the ability to mark fields as optional or required. Right now the graphql definitions that come out have every field as optional, even though I know many of them will cause an error if omitted. I hope given Hasura's crazy fundraising they'll be able to address these points, but so far I'm really happy with the product.
item.author: simonhorlick

item.title: 算法简介👨‍💻 | Introdução a Algoritmos 👨‍💻
item.description:
算法介绍

问题：Fazer um bolo delicioso。

成分：Nós iremos utilizar farinha、leite、ovo、manteiga e 巧克力。

结果：嗯 bolo delicioso de 巧克力。

Outro aspecto que vale ressaltar, em algoritmos utilizamos a lógica para estruturar a maneira ea ideia que são apresentados para resolver determinado problema. Outro aspecto que vale ressaltar, em algoritmos utilizamos a lógica para estruturar a maneira ea ideia que são apresentados para resolver determinado problema。 Já que por sua vez algoritmos é uma sequência de passos, a lógica vai nos ajudar a estruturar os nossos passos em uma ordem que faça sentido, sendo que em nosso exemplo, não há como entregar o bolo sem ao menos te-lô feito, ou se faltar os ingredientes. Já que por sua vez algoritmos é uma sequência de passos, a lógica vai nos ajudar a estruturar os nossos passos em uma ordem que faça sentido, sendo que em nosso exemplo, não ha como entregar o bolo sem ao menos te-lô feito, ou se faltar os 成分。



原文:

Olá dev tudo bem? eu sou Samuel Balena e faço parte do grupo de embaixadores da DIO Campus Experts, e hoje estou aqui para discutir um assunto que é muito importante para a nossa carreira como desenvolvedor. Então vamos lá!

Introdução a Algoritmos

Algoritmos é um assunto básico que está presente em nossa carreira, uma vez que nós desenvolvedores trabalhamos com isso diariamente. Uma definição que podemos utilizar é que, algoritmos se trata de uma sequência de passos que utilizamos para resolver um determinado problema.

Uma receita de bolo por exemplo, pode ser considerado um algoritmo. Vamos ver o exemplo a seguir.

Problema: Fazer um bolo delicioso.

Ingredientes: Nós iremos utilizar farinha, leite, ovo, manteiga e chocolate.

Passos: Primeiro coloque o ovo e farinha e bata até a mistura ficar homogênea. Em seguida, adicione os demais ingredientes, bata por 3 min. Despeje o conteúdo numa assadeira. Aqueça o forno a 180 graus por 5 min. Coloque a assadeira no forno por 40 min.

Resultado: Um bolo delicioso de chocolate.

Outro aspecto que vale ressaltar, em algoritmos utilizamos a lógica para estruturar a maneira e a ideia que são apresentados para resolver determinado problema. Já que por sua vez algoritmos é uma sequência de passos, a lógica vai nos ajudar a estruturar os nossos passos em uma ordem que faça sentido, sendo que em nosso exemplo, não há como entregar o bolo sem ao menos te-lô feito, ou se faltar os ingredientes.
item.author: samuelbalena

item.title: “mangabank.org”消失了。 | The "mangabank.org" has disappeared.
item.description:
神父：
西班牙语：
日本人：
关于什么是漫画银行。

它变成了一个新的非法网站，叫做 13dl.me，但是由于放弃了在线阅读风格，它变成了下载到达类型，所以这篇文章也在板上更新，作为一个新的里程碑。 我试着去做。

过去的董事会直到mangaBank消失

好吧，我一直想知道如何编写Javascript ，所以我使用node.js用 Ja​​vascript 编写了一个程序，看看这个新的非法网站是什么样的。

我花了一段时间，因为我不熟悉Promise ，但他有比Nim更多的Javascript文档，所以没多久程序就开始工作了。

安装node.js并使用npm包管理器安装axios 、 cheerio 。

我使用它就像Ruby中的nokogiri ， python中的bueatifulsoup4 ， go中的nimquery ， Nim中的goquery 。
这些便利选择器有相似的用法和不同，所以如果你不习惯不能正确使用它们，除非你仔细检查它们，即使你不使用它们也可以使用regular expressions进行管理，所以在这种情况下它是即使语言不同也一样。 标准的正则表达式样式可以用相同的方式编写。
它是一个 C++ 库。

许可证 BSD-3-Clause 许可证

什么样的程序，它会列举网站上的内容。
它递归地调用函数，但是我不知道如何用Javascript编写它，但是它起作用了，所以我们记录一下要在SQLite database中列出的内容。
如果您将它与awk一起使用，您也可以使用此基本程序创建SQLite3数据库文件。 使用分隔符{将其设置为csv ，使用SQLite3读取csv并保存，它将是一个数据库文件。

红宝石

如果你编写类似这样的 Ruby 程序代码，

 require 'nokogiri' require 'open-uri' url = 'https://13dl.me/list/popular/' counter = 0 threads = [] def recursive ( counter , url , threads ) html = URI . open ( url ). read doc = Nokogiri :: HTML . parse ( html ) html = nil doc . css ( 'a' ). each do | x | xx = x . attr ( 'title' ) pa = /^Page/ . match? ( " #{ xx } " ) p = /^Prev/ . match? ( " #{ xx } " ) if xx && xx != '' && xx != 'Home' && xx != 'Popular Manga' then unless pa | p then n = /^Next/ . match? ( " #{ xx } " ) link = '' if n then link = x . attr ( 'href' ) threads << Thread . new do recursive ( counter , link , threads ) end else counter += 1 puts " #{ counter } #{ xx } " end end end end doc = nil end recursive ( counter , url , threads ) threads . each ( & :join )

我应该如何db.close() ？

但是，即使是2 ...的情况，似乎即使不关闭也可以写入数据库，所以看来commit是可以的。

老PC写硬盘的情况下，如果你一个一个commit或者重复open和close来写SQLite文件，会牺牲速度，会慢，但是可以在里面建表memory并写入数据。 如果最后将结果写入.db文件，可能会很好，因为您只需要在最后提交即可。

有趣，但non-blocking 。 有追赶的余地，但很难，因为结果不会导致错误。

没有什么特别要解释的。

红宝石 sqlite3

 require 'nokogiri' require 'open-uri' require 'sqlite3' url = 'https://13dl.me/list/popular/' counter = 0 threads = [] SQL =<< EOS create table manga( id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, title text ); EOS db = SQLite3 :: Database . open ( "13dlme.db" ) db . execute ( SQL ) def recursive ( counter , url , threads , db ) html = URI . open ( url ). read doc = Nokogiri :: HTML . parse ( html ) html = nil doc . css ( 'a' ). each do | x | xx = x . attr ( 'title' ) pa = /^Page/ . match? ( " #{ xx } " ) p = /^Prev/ . match? ( " #{ xx } " ) if xx && xx != '' && xx != 'Home' && xx != 'Popular Manga' then unless pa | p then n = /^Next/ . match? ( " #{ xx } " ) link = '' if n then link = x . attr ( 'href' ) threads << Thread . new do recursive ( counter , link , threads , db ) end else counter += 1 puts " #{ counter } #{ xx } " db . execute ( "insert into manga(id,title) values(' #{ counter } ',' #{ xx } ') ;" ) end end end end doc = nil end recursive ( counter , url , threads , db ) threads . each ( & :join ) db . close

作为一个程序，如果应用程序termux或userland在智能手机等上运行， node.js可以作为一个包安装，即使它没有 root，所以它可以工作。

在termux的情况下，您可以通过使用npm package manager安装node.js和axios 、 cheerio 、 sqlite3来编写上述程序，但是userland从选择 OS 开始，但根据 OS，它可能是apt 。 与apk有一些区别，但如果可以安装node.js ，其余的与termux几乎相同。

对于iOS ，我有一个名为iSh的终端仿真器应用程序，但我尚未在 iOS 上对其进行测试。 我没有mac，我有一个旧的iOS iphone，但是我对一家叫apple的公司有生理上的反感，所以我不能轻易触摸它。

在Ruby和python中，在安装库之前根据操作系统存在各种差异，但在node.js的情况下，不会出现这种差异可能是一个优势。 不管javascript好不好，都应该重写，让它只在浏览器中工作。

作为版权管理员，如果您管理的列表中有标题，请记下该程序。

在manga bank的时候， cloudflare进程上的图像数据是不允许从日本以外的IP address访问的，但这次好像不是这样，而且标题也是日文字母。 它可能意识到海外需求，因为它对应于单词标题。

Tomasz Wegrzanowski - Tomasz Wegrzanowski - May 5 5月5日



原文:

Fr:
https://rentry.co/hzpue/

Spanish:
https://rentry.co/agr95

Japanese:
https://crieit.net/posts/Bank-6249a7b59dfd9

About what was a manga bank.

The "manga bank" has disappeared.
It became a new illegal site called 13dl.me, but since the online reading style was abandoned and it became a download reach type, this article was also updated on the board, as a new milestone. I tried to do it.

Past board Until mangaBank disappears

https://crieit.net/boards/manga-B

Well, I've always been wondering how to write Javascript, so I wrote a program in Javascript using node.js to see what this new illegal site looks like.

It took me a while because I wasn't familiar with Promise, but he had more documentation on Javascript than Nim, so it wasn't long before the program worked.

Install node.js and install axios, cheerio with npm package manager.

https://nodejs.org/

https://github.com/axios/axios#installing

https://cheerio.js.org/

A module that axios requests https, it is a return value of Promise type and can process response if the https request is successful.
cheerio uses html text as an object and uses it as a css selector.
I used it like nokogiri in Ruby, like bueatifulsoup4 in python, like goquery in go, like nimquery in Nim.
These convenience selectors have similar usages and are different, so if you are not accustomed to not being able to use them properly unless you examine them carefully, you can manage with regular expressions even if you do not use them, so in that case it is the same even if the language is different. The standard regular expression style can be written in the same way.
go also has a perl-style regular expression module, though it's not a standard library.

RE2 is a fast, safe, thread-friendly alternative to backtracking regular expression engines like those used in PCRE, Perl, and Python. It is a C++ library.

License BSD-3-Clause license

https://github.com/google/re2/

1. Base 

const cheerio = require('cheerio'), axios = require('axios');
// url = `https://13dl.me/`; var url = `https://13dl.me/list/popular/`; var counter = 0; function recursive(url){ let temp_url = url; axios.get(url) .then((response) => { if (response.status == 200){ let $ = cheerio.load(response.data); $('a').each(function (i, e) { let title = $(e).attr('title'); let link = $(e).attr('href'); if (title !== undefined){ let h = /^Home/.test(title), po = /^Popular\s/.test(title), p = /^Prev/.test(title), pa = /^Page/.test(title), n = /^Next/.test(title); if (h || po || p || pa || n || title === ``){ //unless } else { counter++; console.log(counter + `{` + title +`{` + link);
// console.log(counter,title);
// console.log(`____________________`); } } if (title === `Next`){ url = link;
// recursive(url); } }) if (url !== temp_url){ recursive(url); } } }).catch(function (e) { console.log(e); recursive(url); });
} recursive(url);

What kind of program is, it enumerates the contents on the site.
It call the function recursively, but I didn't know how to write it in Javascript, but it worked, so let's record the contents to be listed in the SQLite database.
If you use it with awk, you can create SQLite3 database files with this base program as well. Set it to csv with the delimiter {, read csv with SQLite3 and save it, and it will be a database file.

Ruby

If you write Ruby program code similar to this,

require 'nokogiri'
require 'open-uri' url = 'https://13dl.me/list/popular/'
counter = 0
threads = [] def recursive(counter,url,threads) html = URI.open(url).read doc = Nokogiri::HTML.parse(html) html = nil doc.css('a').each do |x| xx = x.attr('title') pa = /^Page/.match?("#{xx}") p = /^Prev/.match?("#{xx}") if xx && xx !='' && xx !='Home' && xx !='Popular Manga' then unless pa | p then n = /^Next/.match?("#{xx}") link = '' if n then link = x.attr('href') threads << Thread.new do recursive(counter,link,threads) end else counter += 1 puts "#{counter} #{xx}" end end end end doc = nil
end recursive(counter,url,threads) threads.each(&:join)

2. SQLite3 

const cheerio = require('cheerio'), axios = require('axios');
const sqlite3 = require('sqlite3').verbose(); const db = new sqlite3.Database('13dlme0.db',(err) => { if(err) { return console.error(err.message); } console.log('Connect to SQLite database.');
}); db.serialize(() => { db.run(`CREATE TABLE manga(id interger,title string,link string)`)
}); var url = `https://13dl.me/list/popular/`; var counter = 0;
function recursive(url){ let temp_url = url; axios.get(url) .then((response) => { if (response.status == 200){ let $ = cheerio.load(response.data); $('a').each(function (i, e) { let title = $(e).attr('title'); let link = $(e).attr('href'); if (title !== undefined){ let h = /^Home/.test(title), po = /^Popular\s/.test(title), p = /^Prev/.test(title), pa = /^Page/.test(title), n = /^Next/.test(title); if (h || po || p || pa || n || title === ``){ //unless } else { counter++;
// console.log(counter + ` ** ` + title + ` ** ` + link); db.run(`insert into manga(id,title) VALUES(?,?)`,[counter,title]); console.log(counter,title); console.log(`____________________`); } } if (title === `Next`){ url = link; } }) if (temp_url != url){ recursive(url); }; } }).catch(function (e) { console.log(e); recursive(url); });
}
recursive(url);

db.close () I haven't.

How should I db.close()?

3. SQLite3 + db.close() 

const cheerio = require('cheerio'), axios = require('axios');
const sqlite3 = require('sqlite3').verbose(); const db = new sqlite3.Database('13dlme_test1-1.db',(err) => { if(err) { return console.error(err.message); } console.log('Connect to SQLite database.');
}); db.serialize(() => { db.run(`CREATE TABLE manga(id interger,title string,link string)`)
});
;
var url = `https://13dl.me/list/popular/`;
var counter = 0;
function recursive(url){ const temp_url = url; const promise1 = axios.get(url) const promiseData1 = promise1.then((response) => { if (response.status == 200){ let $ = cheerio.load(response.data); $('a').each(function (i, e) { const title = $(e).attr('title'); const link = $(e).attr('href'); if (title !== undefined){ const h = /^Home/.test(title), po = /^Popular\s/.test(title), p = /^Prev/.test(title), pa = /^Page/.test(title), n = /^Next/.test(title); if (h || po || p || pa || n || title === ``){ //unless } else { counter++;
// console.log(counter + ` ** ` + title + ` ** ` + link); db.serialize(() => { db.run(`insert into manga(id,title) VALUES(?,?)`,[counter,title]); }); console.log(counter,title); console.log(`____________________`); } } if ((title === `Next`) && (link != undefined)){ url = link; } }) } else { console.log(response); } if (temp_url !== url){ return recursive(url);
// `:keep going:`; } else { console.log('Close SQLite database.'); return `:stop:`; } }).catch(function (e) { console.log(e); }); Promise.all([promiseData1]).then((value) => { if (value[0] === `:stop:`){ db.close((err) => { if(err) { console.error(err.message); } }); } });
} recursive(url);

4. async/await 

const cheerio = require('cheerio'), axios = require('axios');
const sqlite3 = require('sqlite3').verbose(); const db = new sqlite3.Database('13dlme_test2.db',(err) => { if(err) { return console.error(err.message); } console.log('Connect to SQLite database.');
}); db.serialize(() => { db.run(`CREATE TABLE manga(id interger,title string,link string)`)
}); var url = `https://13dl.me/list/popular/`; var counter = 0;
const recursive = async (url) => { console.log(url); const temp_url = url; try { const {data} = await axios.get(url); const $ = cheerio.load(data) $('a').each(function (i, e) { const title = $(e).attr('title'); const link = $(e).attr('href'); if (title !== undefined){ const h = /^Home/.test(title), po = /^Popular\s/.test(title), p = /^Prev/.test(title), pa = /^Page/.test(title), n = /^Next/.test(title); if (h || po || p || pa || n || title === ``){ //unless } else { counter++;
// console.log(counter + ` ** ` + title + ` ** ` + link); db.run(`insert into manga(id,title) VALUES(?,?)`,[counter,title]); console.log(counter,title); console.log(`____________________`); } } if ((title === `Next`) && (link != undefined)){ url = link; } }); if (temp_url !== url) { recursive(url); //`:keep going:`; }else{ console.log('Close SQLite database.'); db.close(); //`:stop:`; } } catch(error) { throw error; }
} recursive(url);

However, even in the case of 2 ..., it seems that it can be written to the database even if it is not closed, so it seems that commit is possible.

In the case of an old PC that writes to the hard disk, if you commit one by one or repeat open and close to write to the SQLite file, the speed will be sacrificed and it will be slow, but you can create a table in memory and write the data. If you write the result to the .db file at the end, it may be good because you only have to commit at the end.

Each of the 3 and 4 program codes was written to close the database when all the data was written, but this should not be the case, but it was closed cleanly. Funny, but non-blocking. There is room for pursuit, but it is difficult because the result does not result in an error.

There is nothing special to explain.

Ruby sqlite3 

require 'nokogiri'
require 'open-uri'
require 'sqlite3' url = 'https://13dl.me/list/popular/'
counter = 0
threads = [] SQL =<<EOS 
create table manga( id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, title text );
EOS db = SQLite3::Database.open("13dlme.db")
db.execute(SQL) def recursive(counter,url,threads,db) html = URI.open(url).read doc = Nokogiri::HTML.parse(html) html = nil doc.css('a').each do |x| xx = x.attr('title') pa = /^Page/.match?("#{xx}") p = /^Prev/.match?("#{xx}") if xx && xx !='' && xx !='Home' && xx !='Popular Manga' then unless pa | p then n = /^Next/.match?("#{xx}") link = '' if n then link = x.attr('href') threads << Thread.new do recursive(counter,link,threads,db) end else counter += 1 puts "#{counter} #{xx}" db.execute("insert into manga(id,title) values('#{counter}','#{xx}') ;") end end end end doc = nil
end recursive(counter,url,threads,db)
threads.each(&:join)
db.close

As a program, if the application termux or userland works on a smartphone etc., node.js can be installed as a package even if it is not rooted, so it works.

In the case of termux, you can write the above program by installing node.js and axios, cheerio, sqlite3 with the npm package manager, but userland starts by selecting the OS, but depending on the OS, it may be apt. There are some differences with apk, but if you can install node.js, the rest is almost the same as termux.

For iOS I have a terminal emulator app called iSh, but I haven't tested it on iOS. I don't have a mac and I got an old iOS iphone, but I have a physiological dislike for a company called apple, so I can't touch it easily.

In Ruby and python, there are various differences depending on the OS before installing the library, but in the case of node.js, it may be an advantage that such a difference does not occur. Whether javascript is good or not, it should be rewritten so that it works only in the browser.

As a copyright manager, if there is a title in the list that you manage, take down the procedure.

When it was a manga bank, the image data on the cloudflare process was not allowed to be accessed from outside Japan by IP address, but this time it seems that it is not so, and the title is also in Japanese in alphabet. It may be conscious of overseas demand because it corresponds to the word title.

Tomasz Wegrzanowski - May 5

item.title: Rails 6 中的神奇批量插入 | Magical Bulk Insertion in Rails 6
item.description:
在现代应用程序中，我们主要有一个允许用户执行批量操作的要求。 就像插入记录一样。

在第一次拍摄时，您会想出这个版本

问题区域

那么黑客呢？ 上面的代码是多个sql insert 调用的经典情况，每个调用一个电影。 这会产生缓慢的性能，最终导致糟糕的代码！

如果您在 Rails 5 和更早的版本上运行，您可能会面临编写原始自定义 SQL 或使用像activerecord-import这样的 gem 是您留下的最后选择的情况。

但是一旦你在这里启动并运行，就有一种方法适合你，开箱即用，即insert_all

选择你的魔杖，让我们开始吧！

此处的文档： https ://apidock.com/rails/v6.0.0/ActiveRecord/Persistence/ClassMethods/insert_all

你觉得有用吗？ 让我知道内部评论！
关注我，了解更多有趣的小吃。



原文:

In modern apps we mostly have a requirement which allows user to perform bulk actions. Like insertion of records is the one.

On the first take you would come up with this version

Problematic Region

So what the hack? Above code is a classic situation of multiple sql insert calls, each for a movie creation. This yields slow performance, eventually bad code!

Workaround in Oldies

If you're running on Rails 5 and older versions, you might face the situtation where writing raw customized SQL or using gems like activerecord-import is the final option you left with.

Magic of Rails 6

But once you're up and running here, there is a method for you, built out of the box namely insert_all

Pick your wand and lets get started!

*We are discussing a Car model here

Documentation here: https://apidock.com/rails/v6.0.0/ActiveRecord/Persistence/ClassMethods/insert_all

Do you find it useful? Let me know inside comments!
Follow me for more interesting snacks.

item.title: 弥史馆 | Mi Historia
item.description:
发表于天吾的想法

我们是无服务器的！ - - May 9 5月9日



原文:

MyLittleViruzbx1

Posted on May 10

Tengo una idea

Realizar un tipo: Comic, tal vez un anime no lo se!!!

En el cual tenga un personaje y hable solo sobre temas de tecnologia, hacking, y plasmar mi historia de como aprendi a programar tengo varias ideas en mi cabeza, seria una gran historia...

La aventura de un chico que se adentra en un nuevo mundo muy diferente a la realidad, lleno de tecnologia el cual empieza a programar solo y lo busca una persona la cual se vuelve su menthor el chico al ser entrenado(capacitado) logra aprender!
pero no lo suficiente para poder competir con los demas.

En esta historia nos adentraremos en la vida de "Mylittleviruz" su aventura por el cibermundo y las divertidas y comicas historias con relatos que pasaron en la vida real ciberataques y la historia de como se volvio hacker buscando ser un fullstack se ve inmerso en otro mundo totalmente diferente al que tenia planteado.

Quiero que sea una historia que atrape a la persona que lo vea y se manifieste la historia de internet en el... y las aventuras de varias personas que ha programado y logrado cosas y asi poder hacerlo muy popular entre los informaticos.

...esta historia continuara 2021/05/09 Feliz dia de las madres tambien xD

item.author: krishnaagarwal

item.title: 成对的钩子图案 | The paired hook pattern
item.description:
在使用 React 和 TypeScript 多年之后，我看到了很多组件开发模式，但到目前为止，我还没有看到像“配对钩子模式”那样对函数组件有效的模式。 首先，让我们使用一个经典的： Counter组件。

一个简单的例子

首先我们编写一个无状态组件：

 const Counter = ({ count , onDecrement , onIncrement }) => ( <> < span > { count } </ span > < button onClick = { onIncrement } > + </ button > < button onClick = { onDecrement } > - </ button > </> );

而当我们使用它的时候，我们需要为它创建一个状态：

 const App = () => { const [ count , setCount ] = useState ( 0 ); return ( < Counter count = { count } onDecrement = { () => setCount ( count - 1 ) } onIncrement = { () => setCount ( count + 1 ) } /> ); };

动态看起来像这样：

第一个问题：重用

无状态组件的问题在于，我们每次使用组件时都需要使用useState钩子，这对于需要更多属性且遍布整个应用程序的组件来说可能会很烦人。

因此，将状态直接放在组件中是很常见的。 这样做我们不需要每次使用它时都有一个状态，所以我们的Counter组件会变成这样：

 const Counter = ({ initialCount = 0 , step = 1 }) => { const [ count , setCount ] = useState ( initialCount ); return ( <> < span > { count } </ span > < button onClick = { () => setCount ( count + step ) } > + </ button > < button onClick = { () => setCount ( count - step ) } > - </ button > </> ); };

然后使用它，我们想使用多少次就多少次，而不必为每个创建一个状态：

 const App = () => ( <> < Counter /> < Counter /> < Counter /> </> );

然后动态看起来像这样：

第二个问题：数据流

现在，这很好，直到我们想从父元素中知道计数器元素的当前状态。 所以你可能会想创造一个像这样的怪物：

 const Counter = ({ initialCount = 0 , step = 1 , onCountChange }) => { const [ count , setCount ] = useState ( initialCount ); useEffect (() => onCountChange ?.( count ), [ count ]); return ( <> < span > { count } </ span > < button onClick = { () => setCount ( count + step ) } > + </ button > < button onClick = { () => setCount ( count - step ) } > - </ button > </> ); };

然后像这样使用它：

 const App = () => { const [ count , setCount ] = useState ( 0 ); return ( <> < span > Current count in Counter: { count } </ span > < Counter onCountChange = { setCount } /> </> ); };

一开始可能并不明显，但我们为每个状态更改引入了副作用，只是为了让父级与子级保持同步，这有两个重要问题：

  1. 状态同时存在于两个地方（父元素和子元素）。
  2. 孩子们正在更新父母的状态，所以我们实际上是在反对单向数据流。

钩子最好的事情之一就是我们创建自己的钩子。 我为这个问题提出的解决方案非常简单，但老实说，我相信可以解决我所见过的绝大多数状态问题。 第一步和我们在这里开始的类似，我们只是创建一个无状态组件：

 const Counter = ({ count , onDecrement , onIncrement }) => ( <> < span > { count } </ span > < button onClick = { onIncrement } > + </ button > < button onClick = { onDecrement } > - </ button > </> );

但是这一次，我们不再要求我们组件的消费者自己弄清楚状态，而是创建了一个与我们的组件一起使用的钩子，我们可以将其useCounter 。 这个钩子的主要要求是它需要返回一个属性与Counter的属性匹配的对象：

 const useCounter = ({ initialCount = 0 , step = 1 } = {}) => { const [ count , setCount ] = useState ( initialCount ); return useMemo ( () => ({ count , onDecrement : () => setCount ( count - step ), onIncrement : () => setCount ( count + step ), }), [ count , step ], ); };

这使得现在我们几乎可以将它用作有状态的组件：

 const App = () => { const counterProps = useCounter (); return < Counter { ... counterProps } />; };

但我们也可以将其用作无状态组件：

 const App = () => < Counter count = { 42 } />;

而且我们不再有访问状态的限制，因为状态实际上是在父级中。

 const App = () => { const { count , ... counterProps } = useCounter (); return ( <> < span > Current count in Counter: { count } </ span > < Counter { ...{ count , ... counterProps } } /> </> ); };

然后动态看起来像这样：

通过这种方法，我们真正使我们的组件可重用，因为它不需要上下文或基于副作用的奇怪回调或类似的东西。 我们只有一个很好的纯无状态组件，如果我们想控制任何特定的属性，我们可以直接或部分传递一个钩子。

配对模式的问题（和解决方案）

配对钩子方法的主要问题是，现在我们需要为每个具有某种状态的组件创建一个钩子，当我们只有一个组件时这很好，但是当我们有多个相同类型的组件时就变得棘手了（例如有一个Counter组件列表）。

你可能会想做这样的事情：

 const App = ({ list }) => ( <> { list . map ( initialCount => { const counterProps = useCounter ({ initialCount }); return < Counter { ... counterProps } />; }) } </> );

但是这种方法的问题是你违反了钩子的规则，因为你在循环中调用了useCounter钩子。 现在，如果您考虑一下，您可以遍历具有自己状态的组件，因此一种可行的解决方案是创建组件的“配对”版本，它会为您调用钩子：

 const PairedCounter = ({ initialCount , step , ... props }) => { const counterProps = useCounter ({ initialCount , step }); return < Counter { ... counterProps } { ... props } />; }; // And then... const App = ({ list }) => ( <> { list . map ( initialCount => ( < PairedCounter initialCount = { initialCount } /> )) } </> );

这种方法看起来类似于有状态方法（本文中的第二个示例），但更加灵活和可测试。 我们的另一种方法是为每个项目创建一个组件上下文，而不必自己编写组件，为此，我创建了一个小函数，我在 npm 中发布了一个名为react-pair的函数：

这个函数很简单，你可以自己写，唯一的区别是我在测试它，添加 devtools 集成，并为你使用 TypeScript 输入。 您可以在此处查看来源。 用法很简单， react-pair提供了一个pair函数，你可以用它来创建一个组件，让你在组件上下文中访问钩子（不破坏钩子的规则）：

 import { pair } from " react-pair " ; import { useCounter } from " ./useCounter " ; const PairedCounter = pair ( useCounter ); const Component = ({ list }) => ( < ul > { array . map (( initialCount , index ) => ( < PairedCounter key = { index } > { usePairedCounter => { const counterProps = usePairedCounter ({ initialCount }); return < Counter { ... counterProps } />; } } </ PairedCounter > )) } </ ul > );

需要说明的是，您不需要使用react-pair来实现这一点，您只需手动创建一个新的有状态组件，只需将钩子与组件配对即可。

无论您是否使用 util，生成的动态看起来像这样：

编写一个无状态组件，设计为独立工作。
  • 编写一个自定义钩子以与该组件配对。
  • 使用带有钩子的组件来获得有状态的体验。
  • 使用没有钩子的组件来获得无状态体验。
  • 使用仅具有来自钩子的几个属性的组件以获得混合体验。
  • 循环时使用 util 或 wrapper 组件。

    • 结束的想法

    我已经使用这种模式有一段时间了，到目前为止我没有发现任何阻塞问题，所以我邀请你在你的一个项目中尝试它并告诉我它是如何进行的！



    原文:

    After years of working with React and TypeScript, I've seen a lot of patterns for component development, but so far I didn't see one that works as good for function components as the "paired hook pattern". To get started let's use a classic: The Counter component.

    A simple example

    First we write a stateless component:

    const Counter = ({ count, onDecrement, onIncrement }) => ( <> <span>{count}</span> <button onClick={onIncrement}>+</button> <button onClick={onDecrement}>-</button> </>
);

    And when we use it, we need to create a state for it:

    const App = () => { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ( <Counter count={count} onDecrement={() => setCount(count - 1)} onIncrement={() => setCount(count + 1)} /> );
};

    The dynamic looks something like this:

    The first problem: Reuse

    The problem with the stateless component is that we need to use the useState hook every time we use the component, which might be annoying for components that require more properties and are all over your app.

    So, is pretty common to just put the state directly in the component. Doing this we don't need to have a state every time we use it, so then our Counter component changes to something like this:

    const Counter = ({ initialCount = 0, step = 1 }) => { const [count, setCount] = useState(initialCount); return ( <> <span>{count}</span> <button onClick={() => setCount(count + step)}>+</button> <button onClick={() => setCount(count - step)}>-</button> </> );
};

    And then to use it, as many times as we want without having to create a state for each:

    const App = () => ( <> <Counter /> <Counter /> <Counter /> </>
);

    The dynamic then looks like this:

    The second problem: Data flow

    Now, that's great until we want to know the current state of the counter element from the parent element. So you might be tempted to create a monster like this one:

    const Counter = ({ initialCount = 0, step = 1, onCountChange }) => { const [count, setCount] = useState(initialCount); useEffect(() => onCountChange?.(count), [count]); return ( <> <span>{count}</span> <button onClick={() => setCount(count + step)}>+</button> <button onClick={() => setCount(count - step)}>-</button> </> );
};

    And then use it like this:

    const App = () => { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ( <> <span>Current count in Counter: {count}</span> <Counter onCountChange={setCount} /> </> );
};

    It might not be obvious at first, but we are introducing side effects to every state change just to keep the parent in sync with the children, and this has two significant issues:

    1. The state is living in two places at once (the parent element and the children).
    2. The children are updating the state of the parent, so we are effectively going against the one-way data flow.

    One of the best things about hooks is when we create our own. The solution I propose for this issue is quite simple, but I honestly believe solves the vast majority of issues with state I've seen around. The first step is similar to what we had at the beginning here, we just create a stateless component:

    const Counter = ({ count, onDecrement, onIncrement }) => ( <> <span>{count}</span> <button onClick={onIncrement}>+</button> <button onClick={onDecrement}>-</button> </>
);

    But this time, instead of requiring the consumers of our component to figure out the state themselves, we create a hook that goes together with our component, we can call it useCounter. The main requirement for this hook is that it needs to return an object with properties matching the properties of Counter:

    const useCounter = ({ initialCount = 0, step = 1 } = {}) => { const [count, setCount] = useState(initialCount); return useMemo( () => ({ count, onDecrement: () => setCount(count - step), onIncrement: () => setCount(count + step), }), [count, step], );
};

    What this enables is that now we can use it almost as a stateful component:

    const App = () => { const counterProps = useCounter(); return <Counter {...counterProps} />;
};

    But also we can use it as a stateless component:

    const App = () => <Counter count={42} />;

    And we no longer have limitations accessing the state, because the state is actually in the parent.

    const App = () => { const { count, ...counterProps } = useCounter(); return ( <> <span>Current count in Counter: {count}</span> <Counter {...{ count, ...counterProps }} /> </> );
};

    The dynamic then looks something like this:

    With this approach, we are truly making our component reusable by not making it require a context or weird callbacks based on side effects or anything like that. We just have a nice pure stateless component, with a hook that we can pass directly or just partially if we want to take control of any property in particular.

    The name "paired hook" then comes from providing a hook with a stateless component that can be paired to it.

    A problem (and solution) with the paired pattern

    The main issue the paired hook approach has is that now we need a hook for every component with some kind of state, which is fine when we have a single component, but becomes tricky when we have several components of the same type (like for example having a list of Counter components).

    You might be tempted to do something like this:

    const App = ({ list }) => ( <> {list.map(initialCount => { const counterProps = useCounter({ initialCount }); return <Counter {...counterProps} />; })} </>
);

    But the problem with this approach is that you're going against the rules of hooks because you're calling the useCounter hook inside a loop. Now, if you think about it, you can loop over components that have their own state, so one viable solution is to create a "paired" version of your component, which calls the hook for you:

    const PairedCounter = ({ initialCount, step, ...props }) => { const counterProps = useCounter({ initialCount, step }); return <Counter {...counterProps} {...props} />;
}; // And then...
const App = ({ list }) => ( <> {list.map(initialCount => ( <PairedCounter initialCount={initialCount} /> ))} </>
);

    This approach seems similar to the stateful approach (the second example in this article) but is way more flexible and testable. The other approach we have is to create a component context for every item without having to write a component ourselves, and for that, I created a small function that I published in npm called react-pair:

    The function is so simple, you could write it yourself, the only difference is that I'm testing it, adding devtools integration, and typing with TypeScript for you. You can check the source here. The usage is quite simple, react-pair provides a pair function that you can use to create a component that gives you access to the hook in a component context (without breaking the rules of hooks):

    import { pair } from "react-pair";
import { useCounter } from "./useCounter"; const PairedCounter = pair(useCounter); const Component = ({ list }) => ( <ul> {array.map((initialCount, index) => ( <PairedCounter key={index}> {usePairedCounter => { const counterProps = usePairedCounter({ initialCount }); return <Counter {...counterProps} />; }} </PairedCounter> ))} </ul>
);

    Just to be clear, you don't need to use react-pair to achieve this, you can just create a new stateful component by hand, that just pairs the hook with the component.

    Either if you use the util or not, the resulting dynamic looks something like this:

    TL;DR

    • Write a stateless component, designed to work in isolation.
    • Write a custom hook to be paired with that component.
    • Use the component with the hook for a stateful experience.
    • Use the component without the hook for a stateless experience.
    • Use the component with just a few properties from the hook for a mixed experience.
    • Use an util or a wrapper component when looping.

    Closing thoughts

    I've been using this pattern for a while now and so far I didn't found any blocking issues with it, so I invite you to try it out in one of your projects and tell me how it goes!
item.author: lukeshiru

item.title: 介绍 Mayfield + DEV 讨论系列 | Introducing the Mayfield + DEV discussion series
item.description:
嘿开发社区！

在接下来的两周内，我们将与我们在Mayfield的 DEV 合作伙伴合作举办每日系列讨论会。

自然地，我们觉得一些#discuss线程是完美的出路。

从今天晚些时候开始，我们将在接下来的两周内每个工作日发布一个讨论（总共 10 个帖子）。 它们只是普通的讨论线程，就像每天在 DEV 上发布的那样，但我们希望得到一些很好的答案来深入研究和更广泛地分享。

我们非常期待人们有什么要说的！ 寻找今天晚些时候发布的第一个帖子。

快乐编码！



原文:

Hey DEV Community!

Over the next two weeks, we will be hosting a daily discussion series in collaboration with our DEV partners at Mayfield.

Mayfield is deeply invested in dev-centric companies such as HashiCorp — and they came to us hoping to learn from the community on topics such as the best perceived companies to work for and to get into the nuance of what developers need and want most out of their careers these days. Naturally, we felt like some #discuss threads were the perfect outlet.

Starting later today, we will be posting one discussion per weekday every for the next two weeks (10 posts total). They are just normal discussion threads, like are posted every day here on DEV, but we hope to get some great answers to dive into and share more broadly.

We are very much looking forward to what people have to say! Look for the first post coming out later today.

Happy coding!
