item.title: 如何基于SeaTunnel的ClickHouse同步百亿数据？ | How to synchronize tens of billions of data based on SeaTunnel's ClickHouse?
item.description:
作者 | 编辑 | 测试工程师冯秀兰

对于导入数十亿批次的数据，传统的 JDBC 方式在一些海量数据同步场景中表现不佳。 为了更快地写入数据，Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) 刚刚发布了 2.1.1 版本，提供对 ClickhouseFile-Connector 实现 Bulk load 数据写入的支持。

批量加载意味着将大量数据同步到目标数据库。 在 Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) April Meetup 上，Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) 贡献者范佳分享了《基于 SeaTunnel 的 ClickHouse 批量加载实现》的主题，详细讲解了 ClickHouseFile 高效处理海量数据的实现原理和流程.

感谢测试工程师冯秀兰的文章整理！

本演示文稿包含七个部分。

  • 插件能力对比
  • 优化后方向

目前SeaTunnel同步数据到ClickHouse的流程如下：只要SeaTunnel支持数据源，就可以提取、转换（或不转换）数据，直接写入ClickHouse sink连接器，然后通过 JDBC 写入 ClickHouse 服务器。

但是，通过传统 JDBC 写入 ClickHouse 服务器存在一些问题。

首先，现在使用的工具是ClickHouse提供的驱动，通过HTTP实现，但是HTTP在某些场景下实现效率不是很高。 二是数据量大，如果有重复数据或者一次写入的数据量很大，需要生成相应的insert语句，按照传统方式通过HTTP发送到ClickHouse服务器端，逐项或分批解析执行，不允许数据压缩。

最后，还有一个我们经常遇到的问题，就是数据太多可能导致SeaTunnel端OOM，或者是因为太多数据频繁写入服务端而导致服务端挂起。

所以我们想，有没有比 HTTP 更快的发送方式？ 如果可以在SeaTunnel侧进行数据预处理或数据压缩，那么网络带宽压力就会降低，传输速率也会提高。

如果使用 HTTP 传输协议，当数据量过大，批量是微批量发送请求时，HTTP 可能无法处理。

  • 太多的 INSERT 请求可能会给服务器带来太大的压力。 带宽可以处理大量请求，但服务器端并不总是能够承载它们。 在线服务器不仅需要插入数据，更重要的是查询数据可以被其他业务团队使用。 如果由于插入的数据过多导致服务器集群宕机，那么付出的代价是值得的。

    • 针对这些ClickHouse不擅长的场景，我们想知道有没有办法直接在Spark端做数据压缩，写入数据时不增加Server的资源负载，并且具备写入大量数据的能力迅速地？ 所以我们开发了 ClickHouseFile 插件来解决这个问题。

    这两个功能意味着用户可以直接使用本地文件，而无需在 ClickHouse Server 端进行处理。 因为是相同的格式，所以我们在remote或者SeaTunnel端执行的操作生成的数据和server端无缝兼容，可以使用ClickHouse local写入。 第一行：通过Linux管道将数据传递给ClickHouse-local程序的test_table表。

    第二到第五行：创建一个result_table用于接收数据。

    第六行：将数据从 test_table 传递到 result_table。

    第 7 行：定义数据处理的磁盘路径。

    通过调用 Clickhouse-local 组件，使用 Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) 生成数据文件并压缩数据。 通过与服务器通信，生成的数据直接发送到 Clickhouse 的不同节点，然后数据文件可供节点用于查询。

    原始和当前实现的比较。

    原来数据，包括insert语句都是由Spark发给服务器的，服务器做SQL解析，生成并压缩表数据文件，生成对应的文件，创建对应的索引。 如果我们使用 ClickHouse 本地技术，数据文件的生成、文件压缩和索引创建都是由 SeaTunnel 完成的，最终输出的是服务器端的文件或文件夹，同步到服务器，服务器可以查询数据无需额外操作。

    上述过程使数据同步更加高效，这得益于我们对其进行的三项优化。

    首先，数据通过分割从管道传输到ClickHouseFile，这在长度和内存方面施加了限制。 为此，我们通过MMAP技术将ClickHouse连接器即sink端接收到的数据写入一个临时文件，然后ClickHouse本地从临时文件中读取数据生成我们的目标本地文件，以实现增量数据读取的效果，解决了OM问题。

    其次，它支持分片。 如果集群中只生成一个文件或文件夹，则文件只分发到一个节点，这样会大大降低查询的性能。 因此，我们进行切片支持。 用户可以在配置文件夹中设置分片密钥，算法会将数据分成多个日志文件写入不同的集群节点，显着提升读取性能。

    第三个关键优化是文件传输。 目前SeaTunnel支持两种文件传输方式，一种是SCP，其特点是安全、通用、无需额外配置； 另一个是RSYNC，它有点快速和高效，支持断点续传，但需要额外配置，用户可以选择适合自己的方式。

    综上，ClickHouseFile的大致实现流程如下。

    1.caching data to the ClickHouse sink side. 1.缓存数据到ClickHouse sink端。 2.calling ClickHouse-local to generate the file. 2.调用ClickHouse-local生成文件。 3.sending the data to the ClickHouse server. 3.将数据发送到ClickHouse服务器。

    4.Execution of the ATTACH command. 4.ATTACH命令的执行。



    原文:

    Author | Fan Jia, Apache SeaTunnel(Incubating) Contributor
    Editor | Test Engineer Feng Xiulan

    For importing billions of batches of data, the traditional JDBC approach does not perform as well as it should in some massive data synchronization scenarios. To write data faster, Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) has just released version 2.1.1 to provide support for ClickhouseFile-Connector to implement Bulk load data writing.

    Bulk load means synchronizing large amounts of data to the target DB. SeaTunnel currently supports data synchronization to ClickHouse.

    At the Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) April Meetup, Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) contributor Fan Jia shared the topic of "ClickHouse bulk load implementation based on SeaTunnel", explaining in detail the implementation principle and process of ClickHouseFile for efficient processing of large amounts of data.

    Thanks to the test engineer Feng Xiulan for the article arrangement!

    This presentation contains seven parts.

    • State of ClickHouse Sink
    • Scenarios that ClickHouse Sink isn't good at 
    • Introduction to the ClickHouseFile plugin
    • ClickHouseFile core technologies
    • Analysis of ClickHouseFile plugin implementation
    • Comparison of plug-in capabilities
    • Post-optimization directions

    Fan Jia, Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) contributor, Senior Enginee of WhaleOps.

    01 Status of ClickHouse Sink 

    At present, the process of synchronizing data from SeaTunnel to ClickHouse is as follows: as long as the data source is supported by SeaTunnel, the data can be extracted, converted (or not), and written directly to the ClickHouse sink connector, and then written to the ClickHouse server via JDBC. 

    However, there are some problems with writing to the ClickHouse server via traditional JDBC.

    Firstly, the tool used now is the driver provided by ClickHouse and implemented via HTTP, however, HTTP is not very efficient to implement in certain scenarios. The second is the huge amount of data, if there is duplicate data or a large amount of data written at once, it needs to generate the corresponding insert statement and send it via HTTP to the ClickHouse server-side by the traditional method, where it is parsed and executed item by item or in batches, which does not allow data compression.

    Finally, there is the problem we often encounter, i.e. too much data may lead to an OOM on the SeaTunnel side or a server-side hang due to too much data being written to the server-side too often.

    So we thought, is there a faster way to send than HTTP? If data pre-processing or data compression could be done on the SeaTunnel side, then the network bandwidth pressure would be reduced and the transmission rate would be increased.

    02 Scenarios that ClickHouse Sink isn't good at

    1. If the HTTP transfer protocol is used, HTTP may not be able to handle it when the volume of data is too large and the batch is sending requests in micro-batches.

    2. Too many INSERT requests may put too much pressure on the server. The bandwidth can handle a large number of requests, but the server-side is not always able to carry them. The online server not only needs data inserts but more importantly, the query data can be used by other business teams. If the server cluster goes down due to too much-inserted data, it is more than worth the cost.

    03 ClickHouse File core technologies

    In response to these scenarios that ClickHouse is not good at, we wondered is there a way to do data compression right on the Spark side, without increasing the resource load on the Server when writing data, and with the ability to write large amounts of data quickly? So we developed the ClickHouseFile plugin to solve the problem.

    The key technology of the ClickHouseFile plugin is ClickHouse -local. ClickHouse-local mode allows users to perform fast processing of local files without having to deploy and configure a ClickHouse Server. C lickHouse-local uses the same core as ClickHouse Server, so it supports most features as well as the same format and table engine.

    These two features mean that users can work directly with local files without having to do the processing on the ClickHouse Server side. Because it is the same format, the data generated by the operations we perform on the remote or SeaTunnel side is seamlessly compatible with the server-side and can be written to using ClickHouse local. ClickHouse local is the core technology for the implementation of ClickHouseFile, which allows for implementing the ClickHouse file connector.

    ClickHouse local core is used in the following ways.

    First line: pass the data to the test_table table of the ClickHouse-local program via the Linux pipeline.

    Lines two to five: create a result_table for receiving data.

    The sixth line: pass data from test_table to the result_table.

    Line 7: Define the disk path for data processing.

    By calling the Clickhouse-local component, the Apache SeaTunnel (Incubating) is used to generate the data files and compress the data. By communicating with the Server, the generated data is sent directly to the different nodes of Clickhouse and the data files are then made available to the nodes for the query.

    Comparison of the original and current implementations.

    Originally, the data, including the insert statements was sent by Spark to the server, and the server did the SQL parsing, generated and compressed the table data files, generated the corresponding files, and created the corresponding indexes. If we use ClickHouse local technology, the data file generation, file compression and index creation are done by SeaTunnel, and the final output is a file or folder for the server-side, which is synchronized to the server and the server can queries the data without additional operations.

    04 Core technical points

    The above process makes data synchronization more efficient, thanks to three optimizations we have made to it.

    Firstly, the data is transferred from the pipeline to the ClickHouseFile by the division, which imposes limitations in terms of length and memory. For this reason, we write the data received by the ClickHouse connector, i.e. the sink side, to a temporary file via MMAP technology, and then the ClickHouse local reads the data from the temporary file to generate our target local file, in order to achieve the effect of incremental data reading and solve the OM problem.

    Secondly, it supports sharding. If only one file or folder is generated in a cluster, the file is distributed to only one node, which will greatly reduce the performance of the query. Therefore, we carry out slicing support. Users can set the key for slicing in the configuration folder, and the algorithm will divide the data into multiple log files and write them to different cluster nodes, significantly improving the read performance.

    The third key optimization is file transfer. Currently, SeaTunnel supports two file transfer methods, one is SCP, which is characterized by security, versatility, and no additional configuration; the other is RSYNC, which is somewhat fast and efficient and supports breakpoint resume, but requires additional configuration, users can choose between the way suits their needs.

    05 Plugin implementation analysis

    In summary, the general implementation process of ClickHouseFile is as follows.

    1.caching data to the ClickHouse sink side. 2.calling ClickHouse-local to generate the file. 3.sending the data to the ClickHouse server.

    4.Execution of the ATTACH command.
item.author: seatunnel

item.title: OOP 的科学证据是大规模精神病 | The scientific proof of that OOP is a mass psychosis
item.description:
由于我之前的文章产生了 100 多条评论、9,500 次页面浏览量，以及一些愤怒地评论要求我的陈述证明的人，我想我会证明这一点。 请注意，我试图通过说明“证明就在您的代码中”来解释 OOP 的证明是如何成为软件开发大规模精神病的 - 但是，显然没有人相信我，所以让我用两个简短的代码片段来说明我的意思做的基本一样。

超λ

 join fork http.get:"https://gaiasoul.com" fork http.get:"https://servergardens.com"

对于我们这些没有偏见的人来说，上述内容应该是不言自明的，足以作为证据。 但是，如果您仍然觉得很难看证明，请向孩子（尚未接触 OOP）解释多线程的基础知识，然后解释 HTTP 和从其他服务器检索文档的基础知识，并询问孩子“哪个最容易阅读” ？

如果您仍有疑问，请意识到上述 C++ 代码是由评论者提供的，以证明 C++ “与函数式编程语言一样好” 。 然后意识到没有m_Factories变量的实例化。 假设代码使用公认的编码标准，假设它是某种成员变量可能是安全的，这意味着它声明的类没有显示，成员变量本身也没有显示，所以假设 (完整）代码示例可能至少是上面 C++ 示例中所示大小的 3/4 倍。 当然，C++ 示例也有大量额外的不必要的构造，例如类、类型、构造函数，可能是析构函数，甚至可能（叹息！）复制构造函数等等——这些额外开销中的大部分当然是额外不必要的它与所有 OOP 语言共享的开销。 但是，即使忽略缺少的 C++ 部分，以下内容显然是正确的……

然后意识到上面的Hyperlambda代码片段可以直接粘贴到“Eval”菜单项中并立即执行，这意味着它是一个 100% 完美完整的示例。 您可以在本文的封面图片中看到一些内容。 最后，还要意识到所有实际问题的 Hyperlambda 片段与 C++ 版本一样快，因为 99.999999% 的时间都花在等待 IO 上，并且 Hyperlambda 片段是 100% 完全async的，因此具有相同的特征就 CPU 和 RAM 使用而言，它的 C++ 等价物。 因此，即使 C++ 有时可能比 Hyperlambda 更快，但 C++ 为您带来的速度性能提升在 99% 的情况下完全无关紧要，除非您正在开发硬件驱动程序或类似的东西。

事实是，我可以将 95% 的现有 OOP 代码替换为 20% 的 Hyperlambda 代码。 如果您使用的 C++ 可能只有 Hyperlambda 代码的 5% - 而且对于所有实际问题，它会同样快，消耗相同数量的内存，并且没有更多的 CPU - 假设您正在创建 Web 应用程序当然，访问某种数据库，与其他 HTTP 服务集成。 但是，OOP 是软件开发人员集体精神病的证据确实在您的代码中，所以为了清楚起见，让我再次重复代码......

超λ

 join fork http.get:"https://gaiasoul.com" fork http.get:"https://servergardens.com"

真的，反对代码实际上是徒劳的......：/

顺便说一句，如果您此时要向我展示 Python、Ruby 或 PHP 代码，请意识到 Hyperlambda 可能（至少）比所有这些语言都快一个数量级......

证明的解释

如果你还是不明白，试着想象一个软件开发菜鸟看着这两个代码片段，问问题。 在理解这两个代码片段之前，菜鸟需要问多少问题。 那么，Hyperlambda 版本将如下所示。

  1. 冒号是怎么回事: ？
  2. 空格怎么了？
  3. 什么是叉子？
  4. 什么是加盟？
  5. 什么是网址？

什么是常量？

  • 分配如何工作？
  • 双冒号::是怎么回事？
  • 什么是加盟？
  • 为什么是括号？
  • 为什么m_Factories以m_开头？
  • 怎么了. 在m_factories之后？
  • 为什么我们不能使用. 在HTTP之后， ->部分是什么？
  • 分号是怎么回事？

    • 事实是，一旦您将 C++ 分解为在使用该语言变得富有成效之前需要得到答案的问题，C++ 只不过是“美化的正则表达式” ，将数百万个字符捆绑​​在一起，因此只需更改其中一个字符，通常会对您的整个程序产生影响。 大多数其他 OOP 语言也是如此......



    原文:

    Since my previous article generated 100+ comments, 9,500 page views, and some people commenting in anger demanding proof of my statements, I figured I'd prove it. Notice, I tried to explain how the proof of that OOP is a software development mass psychosis by stating "the proof is in your code" - However, nobody apparently believed me, so let me illustrate what I meant by that with two short code snippets doing basically the same.

    C++ 

    const auto ret = Tasks::join(
   m_Factories.HTTP->GET("https//gaiasoul.com"),
   m_Factories.HTTP->GET("https://servergardens.com")
).Start();

    Hyperlambda 

    join
   fork
      http.get:"https://gaiasoul.com"
   fork
      http.get:"https://servergardens.com"

    For those of us without bias the above should really be self explanatory and enough as proof. However, if you still find it difficult to see the proof, explain the basics of multi threading to a child (who's still not been exposed to OOP), then explain the basics of HTTP and retrieving documents from other servers, and ask the child "which is the easiest to read"?

    If you're still in doubt, please realise that the above C++ code was supplied by a commenter as proof of that C++ was "just as good as functional programming languages". Then realise there's no instantiation of the m_Factories variable. Assuming the code is using the accepted coding standards, it's probably safe to assume it's a member variable of some sort, implying the class where it's declared is not shown, the member variable itself is also not shown, so it's probably safe to assume the (complete) code example would probably be at least 3/4 times as large as illustrated in the C++ example above. The C++ example of course also has tons of additional unnecessary constructs, such as classes, types, constructors probably, possibly destructors, maybe even (sigh!) copy constructors, etc, etc, etc - Most of this additional overhead of course being additional unnecessary overhead it shares with all OOP languages out there. However, even ignoring the missing C++ parts, the following is obviously true ...

    The Hyperlambda example is obviously 100 times more readable!

    Then realise the Hyperlambda code snippet above can literally be pasted into the "Eval" menu item and immediately executed, implying it's a 100% perfectly complete example. Something you can see in the cover image for this article. Then finally, also realise that the Hyperlambda snippet for all practical concerns is just as fast as the C++ version, since 99.999999% of the time is spent waiting for IO, and the Hyperlambda snippet is 100% perfectly async, and hence shares the same traits in regards to CPU and RAM usage as its C++ equivalent. So even though C++ might sometimes be faster than Hyperlambda, the speed performance gains C++ gives you would in 99% of the times be completely irrelevant unless you're developing drivers for hardware or something similar.

    Facts are, I can replace 95% of your existing OOP code, with 20% as much Hyperlambda code. If you're using C++ probably as little as 5% as much Hyperlambda code - And it'll for all practical concerns be just as fast, consume the same amount of memory, and no more CPU - Assuming you're creating web apps of course, accessing some sort of database, integrating with other HTTP services. However, the proof of that OOP is a software developer mass psychosis is literally in your code, so let me repeat the code once more for clarity reasons ...

    C++ 

    const auto ret = Tasks::join(
   m_Factories.HTTP->GET("https//gaiasoul.com"),
   m_Factories.HTTP->GET("https://servergardens.com")
).Start();

    Hyperlambda 

    join
   fork
      http.get:"https://gaiasoul.com"
   fork
      http.get:"https://servergardens.com"

    Really, arguing against the code is literally futile ... :/

    BTW, in case you're about to show me Python, Ruby, or PHP code at this point, please realise that Hyperlambda is probably (at least) one order of magnitude faster than all of these languages ...

    The explanation of the proof

    If you still don't get it, try to imagine a software development noob looking at these two snippets of code, asking questions. How many questions would the noob need to ask before understanding these two code snippets. Well, the Hyperlambda version would be as follows.

    1. What's with the colon :?
    2. What's with the spaces?
    3. What's a fork?
    4. What's a join?
    5. What's a URL?

    The C++ equivalent would resemble the following.

    1. What's a const?
    2. What's with the auto thing?
    3. How does assignment work?
    4. What's with the Task?
    5. What's with the double colons ::?
    6. What's a join?
    7. Why the parenthesis?
    8. What's that m_Factories thing?
    9. Why does m_Factories start with m_?
    10. What's with the . after m_factories?
    11. Why can't we use . after HTTP, and what's with the -> part?
    12. What does Start do?
    13. What's with the semicolons?

    Facts are, once you break down C++ into questions required to have an answer to before becoming productive in the language, C++ is little more than "glorified regex", with millions of characters, tied together such that changing just a single one of these characters, typically have repercussions to your entire program. The same is also true for most other OOP languages ...
item.author: polterguy

item.title: 2022 年 5 月 9 日：本周 VS Code 提示 | May 9th, 2022: VS Code Tip of the Week
item.description:
本周的小贴士是照顾我的老同事@citizen428 。 他提到您可以为特定的编程语言设置不同的字体。 因此，例如，在Go中，有些字体非常适合这种语言。

这真的很酷，当我研究这个时，我意识到您可以为特定语言设置各种设置。 查看我自己的设置，我已经为某些事情这样做了。

查看 特定于语言的编辑器设置。

感谢您的提示，Michael 和 Happy VS Coding！

加斯顿丹尼尔森 - 马赫迪·贾法里—— Oct 26 '21 21 年 10 月 26 日

Mohcine BAADI - Mohcine BAADI - Oct 23 '21 21 年 10 月 23 日



原文:

This week's tip of the week is care of my old co-worker @citizen428. He mentioned that you can set a different font for a specific programming language. So, for example, in Go, there are fonts that work really well with this language.

This is really cool, and as I was looking into this, I realized you can set all kinds of settings for a particular language. Looking into my own settings, I had already done this for certain things.

Check out the Language-specific editor settings.

Thanks for the tip, Michael and Happy VS Coding!

P.S.: If you're looking for a community that’s all about VS Code, come on over and say hi at vscodetips.com!

Gastón Danielsen - Oct 27 '21

Mahdi Jafari - Oct 26 '21

Mohcine BAADI - Oct 23 '21

item.title: 在 AWS EC2 实例 (Ubuntu) 上安装 VPN (OpenVPN) 服务器 | Install VPN (OpenVPN) Server on AWS EC2 Instance (Ubuntu)
item.description:
ピメン.ヴィ.ブリタニア

发表于我认为您可以使用 ec2 免费层来创建自己的 vpn 服务器，而不是购买或使用免费的受限 vpn，嗯...

首先，您需要登录到您的 aws 控制台并找到 ec2 服务，然后创建一个新实例（启动实例）：
选择免费套餐实例类型

  • 生成您自己的密钥对（需要与 ssh 连接）
  • 在网络设置上单击edit
    • 单击add security group rule ，然后为自定义 UDP 添加新规则，端口范围 = 1194，源类型来自anywhere
  • 然后在Advanced detail设置滚动到底部，您将找到User data字段，并填写：
    •  #! /bin/bash apt-get update curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/angristan/openvpn-install/master/openvpn-install.sh chmod +x openvpn-install.sh APPROVE_INSTALL=y ENDPOINT=$(curl -4 ifconfig.co) APPROVE_IP=y IPV6_SUPPORT=n PORT_CHOICE=1 PROTOCOL_CHOICE=1 DNS=1 COMPRESSION_ENABLED=n CUSTOMIZE_ENC=n CLIENT=david PASS=1 ./openvpn-install.sh

    创建实例后，您可以使用您的实例key-pair.pem登录到实例，然后您可以安装openvpn-install以生成您的帐户/配置文件 (.ovpn)。

    安装后，您可以通过运行生成文件：

     sudo ./openvpn-install.sh

    您可以将配置文件复制到本地计算机，然后安装 OpenVpn 客户端 (windows) 或 Tunnelblick (MacOs)，然后连接！



    原文:

    ピメン.ヴィ.ブリタニア

    Posted on May 10

    I think you can use ec2 free tier to create your own vpn server instead of buying or using free limited vpn, hm...

    First, you need to login to your aws console and find ec2 service, then create a new instance (launch intance):
    ***

    • Select free tier instance type
    • Generate your own key-pair (required to connect with ssh)
    • On network setting click edit
      • Click add security group rule then add new rule for custom UDP with port range = 1194 and source type from anywhere
    • Then on Advanced detail setting scroll to bottom and you will find User data field, and fill with: 
    #! /bin/bash
apt-get update
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/angristan/openvpn-install/master/openvpn-install.sh

chmod +x openvpn-install.sh

APPROVE_INSTALL=y ENDPOINT=$(curl -4 ifconfig.co) APPROVE_IP=y IPV6_SUPPORT=n PORT_CHOICE=1 PROTOCOL_CHOICE=1 DNS=1 COMPRESSION_ENABLED=n  CUSTOMIZE_ENC=n CLIENT=david PASS=1 ./openvpn-install.sh

    After instance created, you can login into instance with your instance key-pair.pem, then you can install openvpn-install to generate your account/configuration file (.ovpn).

    When installed, you can generate file by running:

    sudo ./openvpn-install.sh

    You can copy the configuration file into your local machine, and install OpenVpn client (windows) or Tunnelblick (MacOs), then CONNECT!!

item.title: #08：建立博客 - 使用 Astro！ | #08: Build a Blog - With Astro!
item.description:
欢迎来到#30DaysOfSWA Week 2, Day 1 ！

在第 1 周，我们介绍了 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序的核心概念——从开发到 API 启用、安全性、配置和部署。

今天，我们开始第 2 周，重点关注 SWA 的实际应用——使用流行的 Web 开发框架和静态站点生成器来构建和部署不同类型的应用程序。 我们首先看一下Astro - 一个采用 BYOF（“自带框架”）方法的现代静态站点生成器。

我们将涵盖的内容

  • 什么是静态站点生成器？
  • 什么是天文？
  • 使用 Astro 构建和部署个人博客
  • 练习：尝试使用教程来部署您的个人博客！

静态站点生成器

术语“静态站点生成器”(SSG) 是指自动为您构建静态网页、使用您的自定义内容或数据填充所选模板的工具。 代码生成对您来说是透明的，即使是非开发人员也可以快速设置自定义静态网站。

静态网站生成是快速、灵活的网站创建的发展趋势。 但它需要一个外部服务来处理托管和构建/部署工作流。 这就是 Azure 静态 Web 应用的用武之地！ 查看SWA 文档，了解有关 Hugo、Jekyll 和 Gatsby 等流行 SSG 的教程。 我们将在本周晚些时候探讨其他一些示例。

你好，阿斯特罗！

然而，今天的帖子将重点关注Astro ，它是静态站点类别中 JavaScript 的后起之秀之一。 自带框架（React、Vue、Svelte、Web 组件）

  • 使用该框架的 UI 组件编写您的网站
  • 以下是 Astro 的简要概述。

    天文 + SWA

    在今天的教程中，我们将逐步完成以下步骤：

    最终结果将如下所示。

    以下是要了解的关键链接：

    然后明天再回来查看新的使用示例和教程——这一次，重点是 React——我们将继续探索 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序的实际应用！

    锻炼

    这里有几个选项可以尝试，以获得更多的实践经验：

    • 克隆模板项目并按照教程构建和部署您的个人博客。
    • 访问Astro 主题页面并尝试使用不同的启动项目，然后将其部署到 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序。

    资源

    查看使用更传统的静态站点生成器的其他教程：



    • 原文:

    Welcome to Week 2, Day 1 of #30DaysOfSWA!!

    In Week 1 we covered core concepts for Azure Static Web Apps - from development to API-enablement, security, configuraton and deployment.

    Today, we kick off Week 2 with a focus on seeing SWA in action - using popular web development frameworks and static site generators to build and deploy different kinds of applications. We kick things off with a look at Astro - a modern static site generator with a BYOF ("Bring your own framework") approach.

    What We'll Cover

    • What is a static site generator?
    • What is Astro?
    • Build & Deploy a Personal Blog with Astro
    • Exercise: Try the tutorial to deploy your personal blog!

    Static Site Generators

    The term "static site generators" (SSG) refers to tools that automatically build static webpages for you, populating a chosen template with your custom content or data. The code generation is transparent to you, making it possible for even non-developers to setup custom static websites quickly.

    Static site generation is a growing trend for fast, flexible website creation. But it requires an external service to handle hosting and build/deploy workflows. This is where Azure Static Web Apps comes in! Check out the SWA documentation for tutorials featuring popular SSGs like Hugo, Jekyll and Gatsby. We'll explore some other examples later this week.

    Hello, Astro!

    Today's post will however focus on Astro, one of the rising stars of JavaScript in the Static Sites category. Astro is a static site builder that works like an SSG but with some novel features that set it apart.

    • Bring your own framework (React, Vue, Svelte, web components)
    • Compose your website using that framework's UI components
    • Astro now renders a fully-static (all HTML, no JavaScript) website

    Astro emphasizes fast performance with less JavaScript - and takes a partial hydration approach that allows it to load the minimal required amount of JavaScript, if and when absolutely necessary. Here's a quick overview of Astro.

    Astro + SWA

    In today's tutorial, we walk through the steps required to:

    The end result will look like this.

    Here are the key links to know:

    Then check back tomorrow for a new usage example and tutorial - this time, with a focus on React - as we continue to explore Azure Static Web Apps in action!

    Exercise

    Here are a couple of options to try, to get more hands-on experience:

    • Clone the template project and follow the tutorial to build and deploy your personal blog.
    • Visit the Astro Themes page and try using a different starter project, then deploying it to Azure Static Web Apps.

    Resources

    Check out these other tutorials using more traditional static site generators:

item.title: 如何做困难的事情 | How to Do Hard Things
item.description:
通往成功的道路不是秘密，这是一个令人不快的事实。 我们大多数人都清楚地知道我们需要做什么来改善我们的生活。 但我们不做这些事情，因为它们很难。 我们更喜欢技巧、公式和技巧，而不是做我们知道最有效的一件事。 几个例子：

  • 我们报名参加课程，而不是从头开始编写网站。
  • 我们做孤立的机器练习，而不是在健身房里举重。
  • 我们忽略了我们的伴侣，而不是进行艰难的交谈。
  • 我们继续时尚饮食，而不是建立健康的生活方式。
  • 我们滚动推特而不是看书。

我们这样做是有原因的，这就是所谓的享乐动机。 人类天生倾向于喜欢感觉良好的事物并避免感觉不好的事物。 这就是我们在网上搜索生产力技巧而不是真正提高生产力的原因。 我们也将这些选择合理化，通常以非常聪明的方式，但在内心深处，我们可以感觉到我们只是在避免做有效但困难的事情。

如何做困难的事情

弄清楚困难是什么

首先，你需要确保你知道在你想要提高的技能或工作中什么是困难但有效的事情。 这应该很容易弄清楚，因为它通常是创建输出的东西。 对于作家来说，就是写作。 对于编码员来说，就是编码。 对于音乐家来说，它正在写一首歌。 对于运动员来说，它正在锻炼身体。

重新连接你的大脑

花更多时间在有效、困难的事情上的诀窍是改变你的想法，让困难的事情看起来不错，而替代的事情看起来很糟糕。 例如，您可以从头开始编写一个网站并真正成为一名更有能力的开发人员，或者您可以注册另一门课程，重新学习您已经知道的内容或您可以轻松查找的内容。 哪一个更好？

找出你的原因

确保你知道你为什么要做这件艰难的事情。 如果你不知道为什么，你很可能很快就会失去动力。 所以找出你的原因并经常提醒自己。 把它写下来，放在你能看到的地方。 你的为什么越强，越好。

为它腾出时间

接下来，做出承诺。 把困难的事情变成习惯。 每天在特定的时间，你都会做这件艰难的事情。 纯粹的专注，没有一心多用。 一开始放轻松，然后逐渐延长你花在这上面的时间。 享受它，也是。 享受磨砺，痛苦，困难。 你是赢家，你可以做到。

抛开疑虑

当你做某件困难的事情时，你的大脑会想方设法阻止它。 你会想查找或找到更有效的方法来完成困难的事情。 在你努力工作的过程中，不要屈服于这些诱人的论点。 在之后或之前做任何你想做的事，但在此期间保持专注。

如果坚持不懈地努力工作，您不仅会在人群中脱颖而出，而且还会增加运气的表面积。 如果您从不发布任何代码，招聘人员将永远不会与您联系并提供您梦寐以求的技术工作。 但如果你努力工作并在公共场合展示这项工作，他们可能会这样做。

任何事情都一样。 如果您通过做艰苦的事情来不断提高自己，那么这将产生的机会数量只会越来越多。 所以你会在你的目标技能上变得更好，并且在这个过程中似乎也会成为一个更幸运的人。 所以不要花太多时间来解决艰巨的任务。 只是做困难的事情。



原文:

It's an inconvenient truth that the road to success isn't a secret. Most of us know exactly what we need to do to improve our lives. But we don't do those things because they are hard. We prefer tricks, formulas, and hacks over doing the one thing that we know is most effective. A few examples:

  • We sign up for a course instead of coding a website from scratch.
  • We do isolated machine exercises instead of heavy lifts in the gym.
  • We ignore our partner instead of having a hard conversation.
  • We go on fad diets instead of building a healthy lifestyle.
  • We scroll Twitter instead of reading a book.

There's a reason we act like this and it's called hedonic motivation. Humans are wired to prefer things that feel good and avoid things that feel bad. That's why we search for productivity tips online instead of actually being productive. We rationalize these choices too, often in very clever ways, but deep down we can feel that we're simply avoiding doing what's effective, but hard.

How to Do the Hard Things

Figure Out What the Hard Thing Is

First, you need to make sure you know what the hard, but effective thing is in the skill or job that you want to improve. This should be easy to figure out, because it's usually the thing that creates the output. For writers, it's writing. For coders, it's coding. For musicians, it's writing a song. For athletes, it's doing a workout.

Rewire Your Brain

The trick to spending more time on the effective, hard things is to change your thinking so the hard thing looks good while the alternative looks bad. For example, you can program a website from scratch and genuinely become a more competent developer or you can sign up for another course that will rehash what you already know or what you could easily have looked up. Which one is better?

Figure Out Your Why

Make sure you know why you want to do this hard thing. If you don't know why, chances are you'll lose motivation pretty quickly. So figure out your why and frequently remind yourself of it. Write it down and put it where you can see it. The stronger your why, the better.

Make Time for It

Next up, make a commitment. Turn the hard thing into a habit. Every day at a particular hour, you will do this one hard thing. Pure focus, no multi-tasking. Take it easy at first and gradually lengthen the amount of time you spend on this. Enjoy it, too. Enjoy the grind, the pain, the difficulty. You're a winner and you can do it.

Leave the Doubts Behind

When you're doing something hard, your brain will try to find ways to stop doing it. You'll want to look things up or find more efficient ways of doing the hard thing. Don't give in to these seductive arguments during your hard work sessions. Do whatever you want after or before, but stay focused during.

If consistently put in the hard work, you will not only stand out from the crowd, but you will also increase your surface area for luck. There's no way a recruiter will ever reach out to you with your dream job in tech if you never publish any code. But if you work hard and show that work in public, they just might.

The same goes for anything. If you keep improving yourself by doing hard things, the number of opportunities that this will generate will only ever go up. So you'll be getting better at your targeted skill and seemingly become a luckier person in the process too. So don't spend too much time working around the hard task. Just do the hard thing instead.
item.author: tdmoor

item.title: 使用 Lambda 函数 URL 编写由 DynamoDB 支持的无服务器应用程序 | Use Lambda Function URL to write a Serverless app backed by DynamoDB
item.description:
这是一个使用 AWS SAM 打包和部署的 Go Lambda 函数

当您只需要一个函数的单个端点（例如用作 webhook）并且不想设置和配置 API 网关时，它真的很有用。 看起来我似乎无法获得足够的它！ 我已经写了几篇关于这个主题的博客文章，其中包括一个使用它来构建无服务器后端然后使用 AWS CDK 部署该解决方案的实际示例。

这是另一篇博文（很短！），演示了如何使用 Lambda 函数 URL 编写一个由 DynamoDB 支持的简单应用程序。 您将能够调用由 Lambda 函数 URL 公开的 API 端点，该端点又将在 DynamoDB 上执行操作（ GetItem 、 PutItem 、 Scan ）。 该函数是使用AWS Go开发工具包中的DynamoDB 包在 Go 中编写的， AWS 无服务器应用程序模型 (SAM)用于快速构建和部署解决方案。

代码在 GitHub 上供您参考

让我们开始吧！ 在继续之前，请确保您已准备好以下内容：

先决条件

部署

首先克隆 Github 存储库并切换到正确的目录：

 git clone https://github.com/abhirockzz/lambda-functionurl-dynamodb-sam-go cd lambda-functionurl-dynamodb-sam-go

使用 AWS SAM 构建应用程序：

现在您已准备好部署应用程序：

出现提示时，输入所需的信息，例如堆栈名称等。请参见下面的示例：

 Configuring SAM deploy ====================== Looking for config file [samconfig.toml] : Not found Setting default arguments for 'sam deploy' ========================================= Stack Name [sam-app]: AWS Region [us-east-1]: #Shows you resources changes to be deployed and require a 'Y' to initiate deploy Confirm changes before deploy [y/N]: y #SAM needs permission to be able to create roles to connect to the resources in your template Allow SAM CLI IAM role creation [Y/n]: y #Preserves the state of previously provisioned resources when an operation fails Disable rollback [y/N]: n DemoFunction Function Url may not have authorization defined, Is this okay? [y/N]: y Save arguments to configuration file [Y/n]: SAM configuration file [samconfig.toml]: SAM configuration environment [default]:

运行sam deploy --guided模式一次并将参数保存到配置文件 (samconfig.toml) 后，以后可以使用sam deploy来使用这些默认值。

如果成功，您现在应该准备好以下内容：

  • 带有 HTTP(S) URL 的 Lambda 函数
  • 具有 Lambda 和 DynamoDB（ PutItem 、 GetItem和Scan ）所需的最低权限的 IAM 角色

请注意 SAM 部署过程的输出。 这包含测试所需的函数的 HTTP URL 端点

测试应用程序

测试集成涉及向 Lambda 函数 URL 发送 HTTP 请求。 此示例使用curl CLI，但您也可以使用其他选项。

将 Lambda 函数 URL 端点导出为环境变量。

 export LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT = <SAM deployment output> eg export LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT = https://qfmpu2n74ik7m2m3ipv3m6yw5a0qiwmp.lambda-url.us-east-1.on.aws/

调用终端节点以在 DynamoDB 表中创建新条目：

 curl -i -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"email":"user1@foo.com", "username":"user-1"}' $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT curl -i -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"email":"user2@foo.com", "username":"user-2"}' $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT

在这两种情况下，您都应该得到 HTTP 201 Created响应。 这表示项目已添加到 DynamoDB 中的users表中。

让我们检索我们刚刚创建的记录的信息（我们通过向 URL 添加query参数（电子邮件）来实现）：

 curl -i $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT ?email = user2@foo.com

要检索您刚刚添加的所有项目：

 curl -i $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT

尝试搜索您尚未添加的项目：

 curl -i $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT ?email = notthere@foo.com

在这种情况下，您将收到HTTP 404 Not Found响应。

最后，尝试插入重复记录：

 curl -i -X POST -d '{"email":"user2@foo.com", "username":"user-2"}' $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT

在这种情况下，Lambda 函数返回 HTTP 409 Conflict ，因为条件表达式( attribute_not_exists(email) ) 可防止现有项目（具有相同的email ）被PutItem调用覆盖。

就是这样！ 您已经尝试了 Lambda 函数公开的所有操作。

清理

完成后，请删除堆栈：

 sam delete --stack-name STACK_NAME

确认堆栈已被删除

aws cloudformation list-stacks --query "StackSummaries[?contains(StackName,'STACK_NAME')].StackStatus"

包起来！

这是一个简短（但希望有用）的教程！ 您可以使用它来快速引导具有 DynamoDB 后端的无服务器应用程序，该后端具有由 Lambda 函数 URL 公开的功能。

快乐编码！



原文:

This is a Go Lambda function that's packaged and deployed using AWS SAM

Lambda Function URL is a relatively new feature (at the time of writing this blog) that provides dedicated HTTP(S) endpoint for your Lambda function. It is really useful when all you need is a single endpoint for your function (e.g. to serve as a webhook) and don't want to setup and configure an API Gateway. Looks like I can't seem to get enough of it! I have written a couple of blog posts on this topic that include a practical example of using it to build a serverless backend and then deploying that solution using AWS CDK.

This is yet another blog post (it's a short one!) that demonstrates how you can use Lambda Function URL to write a simple application backed by DynamoDB. You will be able to invoke an API endpoint exposed by the Lambda Function URL,which in turn will execute operations (GetItem, PutItem, Scan) on DynamoDB. The function is written in Go using the DynamoDB package in AWS Go SDK and AWS Serverless Application Model (SAM) is used to quickly build and deploy the solution.

The code is available on GitHub for your reference

Let's get right to it! Before you move on, make sure you have the following ready:

Pre-requisites

Deployment

Start by cloning the Github repo and change to the right directory:

git clone https://github.com/abhirockzz/lambda-functionurl-dynamodb-sam-go
cd lambda-functionurl-dynamodb-sam-go

Use AWS SAM to build the app:

Now you're ready to deploy the application:

When prompted, enter the required information such as stack name etc. See example below:

Configuring SAM deploy
======================

        Looking for config file [samconfig.toml] :  Not found

        Setting default arguments for 'sam deploy'
        =========================================
        Stack Name [sam-app]: 
        AWS Region [us-east-1]: 
        #Shows you resources changes to be deployed and require a 'Y' to initiate deploy
        Confirm changes before deploy [y/N]: y
        #SAM needs permission to be able to create roles to connect to the resources in your template
        Allow SAM CLI IAM role creation [Y/n]: y
        #Preserves the state of previously provisioned resources when an operation fails
        Disable rollback [y/N]: n
        DemoFunction Function Url may not have authorization defined, Is this okay? [y/N]: y
        Save arguments to configuration file [Y/n]: 
        SAM configuration file [samconfig.toml]: 
        SAM configuration environment [default]:

Once you have run sam deploy --guided mode once and saved arguments to a configuration file (samconfig.toml), you can use sam deploy in future to use these defaults.

If successful, you should now have the following ready:

  • Lambda Function with a HTTP(S) URL
  • A DynamoDB table (called users)
  • IAM Role with minimum required permissions for Lambda as well as DynamoDB (PutItem, GetItem, and Scan)

Note the output from the SAM deployment process. This contains the HTTP URL endpoint for your function required for testing

Test the application

Testing the integration involves sending an HTTP requests to the Lambda Function URL. This example used the curl CLI, but you can also use other options.

Export the Lambda Function URL endpoint as an environment variable.

export LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT=<SAM deployment output>
e.g. export LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT=https://qfmpu2n74ik7m2m3ipv3m6yw5a0qiwmp.lambda-url.us-east-1.on.aws/

Invoke the endpoint to create new entries in the DynamoDB table:

curl -i -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"email":"user1@foo.com", "username":"user-1"}' $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT curl -i -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"email":"user2@foo.com", "username":"user-2"}' $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT

You should get an HTTP 201 Created response in both the cases. This indicates that the items have been added to the users table in DynamoDB.

Let's retrieve the info for the record we just created (we do that by adding a query parameter (email) to the URL):

curl -i $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT?email=user2@foo.com

To retrieve all the items that you just added:

curl -i $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT

Try to search for an item that you haven't yet added:

curl -i $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT?email=notthere@foo.com

You will get back a HTTP 404 Not Found response in this case.

Finally, try to insert a duplicate record:

curl -i -X POST -d '{"email":"user2@foo.com", "username":"user-2"}' $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT

The Lambda function returns an HTTP 409 Conflict in this case since a Condition Expression (attribute_not_exists(email)) prevents an existing item (with the same email) from being overwritten by the PutItem call.

That's it! You have tried all the operations exposed by the Lambda function.

Cleanup

Once you're done, please delete the stack:

sam delete --stack-name STACK_NAME

Confirm the stack has been deleted

aws cloudformation list-stacks --query "StackSummaries[?contains(StackName,'STACK_NAME')].StackStatus"

Wrap up!

This was a short (but hopefully useful) tutorial! You can use this to quickly bootstrap a Serverless app with a DynamoDB backend with functionality exposed by a Lambda Function URL.

Happy coding!
item.author: abhirockzz

item.title: 使用 Lambda 函数 URL 编写由 DynamoDB 支持的无服务器应用程序 | Use Lambda Function URL to write a Serverless app backed by DynamoDB
item.description:
这是一个使用 AWS SAM 打包和部署的 Go Lambda 函数

当您只需要一个函数的单个端点（例如用作 webhook）并且不想设置和配置 API 网关时，它真的很有用。 看起来我似乎无法获得足够的它！ 我已经写了几篇关于这个主题的博客文章，其中包括一个使用它来构建无服务器后端然后使用 AWS CDK 部署该解决方案的实际示例。

  • 带有 HTTP(S) URL 的 Lambda 函数

请注意 SAM 部署过程的输出。 这包含测试所需的函数的 HTTP URL 端点

将 Lambda 函数 URL 端点导出为环境变量。

让我们检索我们刚刚创建的记录的信息（我们通过向 URL 添加query参数（电子邮件）来实现）：

 curl -i $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT ?email = user2@foo.com

要检索您刚刚添加的所有项目：

 curl -i $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT

尝试搜索您尚未添加的项目：

 curl -i $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT ?email = notthere@foo.com

在这种情况下，您将收到HTTP 404 Not Found响应。

最后，尝试插入重复记录：

 curl -i -X POST -d '{"email":"user2@foo.com", "username":"user-2"}' $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT

在这种情况下，Lambda 函数返回 HTTP 409 Conflict ，因为条件表达式( attribute_not_exists(email) ) 可防止现有项目（具有相同的email ）被PutItem调用覆盖。

就是这样！ 您已经尝试了 Lambda 函数公开的所有操作。

清理

完成后，请删除堆栈：

 sam delete --stack-name STACK_NAME

确认堆栈已被删除

aws cloudformation list-stacks --query "StackSummaries[?contains(StackName,'STACK_NAME')].StackStatus"

包起来！

这是一个简短（但希望有用）的教程！ 您可以使用它来快速引导具有 DynamoDB 后端的无服务器应用程序，该后端具有由 Lambda 函数 URL 公开的功能。

快乐编码！



原文:

This is a Go Lambda function that's packaged and deployed using AWS SAM

Lambda Function URL is a relatively new feature (at the time of writing this blog) that provides dedicated HTTP(S) endpoint for your Lambda function. It is really useful when all you need is a single endpoint for your function (e.g. to serve as a webhook) and don't want to setup and configure an API Gateway. Looks like I can't seem to get enough of it! I have written a couple of blog posts on this topic that include a practical example of using it to build a serverless backend and then deploying that solution using AWS CDK.

This is yet another blog post (it's a short one!) that demonstrates how you can use Lambda Function URL to write a simple application backed by DynamoDB. You will be able to invoke an API endpoint exposed by the Lambda Function URL,which in turn will execute operations (GetItem, PutItem, Scan) on DynamoDB. The function is written in Go using the DynamoDB package in AWS Go SDK and AWS Serverless Application Model (SAM) is used to quickly build and deploy the solution.

The code is available on GitHub for your reference

Let's get right to it! Before you move on, make sure you have the following ready:

Pre-requisites

Deployment

Start by cloning the Github repo and change to the right directory:

git clone https://github.com/abhirockzz/lambda-functionurl-dynamodb-sam-go
cd lambda-functionurl-dynamodb-sam-go

Use AWS SAM to build the app:

Now you're ready to deploy the application:

When prompted, enter the required information such as stack name etc. See example below:

Configuring SAM deploy
======================

        Looking for config file [samconfig.toml] :  Not found

        Setting default arguments for 'sam deploy'
        =========================================
        Stack Name [sam-app]: 
        AWS Region [us-east-1]: 
        #Shows you resources changes to be deployed and require a 'Y' to initiate deploy
        Confirm changes before deploy [y/N]: y
        #SAM needs permission to be able to create roles to connect to the resources in your template
        Allow SAM CLI IAM role creation [Y/n]: y
        #Preserves the state of previously provisioned resources when an operation fails
        Disable rollback [y/N]: n
        DemoFunction Function Url may not have authorization defined, Is this okay? [y/N]: y
        Save arguments to configuration file [Y/n]: 
        SAM configuration file [samconfig.toml]: 
        SAM configuration environment [default]:

Once you have run sam deploy --guided mode once and saved arguments to a configuration file (samconfig.toml), you can use sam deploy in future to use these defaults.

If successful, you should now have the following ready:

  • Lambda Function with a HTTP(S) URL
  • A DynamoDB table (called users)
  • IAM Role with minimum required permissions for Lambda as well as DynamoDB (PutItem, GetItem, and Scan)

Note the output from the SAM deployment process. This contains the HTTP URL endpoint for your function required for testing

Test the application

Testing the integration involves sending an HTTP requests to the Lambda Function URL. This example used the curl CLI, but you can also use other options.

Export the Lambda Function URL endpoint as an environment variable.

export LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT=<SAM deployment output>
e.g. export LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT=https://qfmpu2n74ik7m2m3ipv3m6yw5a0qiwmp.lambda-url.us-east-1.on.aws/

Invoke the endpoint to create new entries in the DynamoDB table:

curl -i -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"email":"user1@foo.com", "username":"user-1"}' $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT curl -i -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"email":"user2@foo.com", "username":"user-2"}' $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT

You should get an HTTP 201 Created response in both the cases. This indicates that the items have been added to the users table in DynamoDB.

Let's retrieve the info for the record we just created (we do that by adding a query parameter (email) to the URL):

curl -i $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT?email=user2@foo.com

To retrieve all the items that you just added:

curl -i $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT

Try to search for an item that you haven't yet added:

curl -i $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT?email=notthere@foo.com

You will get back a HTTP 404 Not Found response in this case.

Finally, try to insert a duplicate record:

curl -i -X POST -d '{"email":"user2@foo.com", "username":"user-2"}' $LAMBDA_FUNCTION_URL_ENDPOINT

The Lambda function returns an HTTP 409 Conflict in this case since a Condition Expression (attribute_not_exists(email)) prevents an existing item (with the same email) from being overwritten by the PutItem call.

That's it! You have tried all the operations exposed by the Lambda function.

Cleanup

Once you're done, please delete the stack:

sam delete --stack-name STACK_NAME

Confirm the stack has been deleted

aws cloudformation list-stacks --query "StackSummaries[?contains(StackName,'STACK_NAME')].StackStatus"

Wrap up!

This was a short (but hopefully useful) tutorial! You can use this to quickly bootstrap a Serverless app with a DynamoDB backend with functionality exposed by a Lambda Function URL.

Happy coding!
item.author: abhirockzz

item.title: 面向对象的 Javascript 简介：第 1 部分 | Intro to Object Oriented Javascript: Part 1
item.description:
最初发表于Hashnode

嗨，我的名字是Adhik 👋。 我是一名前端 Web 开发人员。 你可以在Twitter 上找到我。

只要面向对象编程一直存在，有时就很难理解它。 在这篇文章和接下来的文章中，我将尽我所能让这些面向对象的概念更容易理解。

对于刚开始学习 javascript 的人来说，可能熟悉也可能不熟悉 ES6+ 语法。 不用担心。 我们将简要了解 ES6+ 出现之前的版本，以便当您开始使用 ES6+ 语法时，您会毫不费力地理解术语。

我们将学习如何在构造函数的帮助下创建对象。

你还在吗？ 让我们深入了解它。

对象是属性和方法的集合。 例如，汽车具有独特的颜色、品牌、座椅类型（手工缝制或皮革）等属性。 您可以使用加速器启动汽车并加速，您可以使用刹车来停止汽车。 这些不过是 Car 的方法。

我们可以通过许多众所周知的方式创建对象，

  • 使用对象字面量语法

  • 使用 **Object.create() **方法

  • 使用构造函数

  1. 使用对象字面量语法
let audi = { model: "A4", brand: "Audi", color: "Metallic Black" }; let bmw = { model: "Z4", brand: "BMW", color: "Alpine White" };

在上面的示例中，我们创建了一些名为 audi 和 bmw 的变量，并将它们包裹在花括号 ({}) 中。 这就是我们使用对象字面量创建对象的方式，即使用花括号 ({})。 型号、品牌和颜色不过是对象的属性。

  1. 使用 Object.create() 方法
let person = { firstName: "John", lastName: "Doe", age: 42 }; const newPerson = Object.create(person); newPerson.firstName = "Jane"; newPerson.age = 32; console.log(newPerson);

对象从原型继承属性和方法。 简单来说，对象“newPerson”继承了“person”对象的firstName、lastName和age属性，因为我们在创建**“newPerson”**对象时使用了“person”对象的引用。
我们将在下一篇文章中详细讨论原型。

  1. 使用构造函数

在使用上面的对象字面量语法时，我们定义了两个具有相同属性的对象。 在那个例子中，我们指的是汽车的两个不同品牌，但是任何汽车的属性和方法都是一样的，对吧？ 品牌、型号、颜色不同，但属性名称相同。

想象一下，我们必须为每个不同的汽车对象创建 20 多个对象，但它们都具有相同的属性名称，只是它们的值不同。 在这种情况下，为每个品牌创建对象只是重复，而不是保持代码干燥（不要重复自己）。 这就是构造函数发挥作用的地方。

在 JavaScript 中，构造函数在使用 new 关键字创建对象时被调用。 构造函数的目的是创建一个新对象并为任何现有对象属性设置值。

 function Person(firstName, lastName){ this.firstName = firstName; this.lastName = lastName; this.greet = function() { console.log(`Hi, this is ${this.firstName} ${lastName}`) } } // Creating an instance of the Person constructor using the "new" operator const john = new Person("John", "Doe"); john.greet(); //Expected Output: Hi, this is John Doe.

好的，让我们分解这个复杂的构造函数。 我们有一个名为 Person 的函数。 如果你仔细看，你会注意到函数名的首字母是大写的。 当我们定义构造函数或类时（ES6+ 概念，将在后面的文章中讨论），将构造函数或类的首字母大写被认为是一种很好的做法。

在这里，我们创建了构造函数Person的一个实例。 换句话说，上例中的this关键字指的是在创建实例“john”时定义的对象属性。 这就是为什么我们可以使用构造函数的方法greet ，因为它在这种情况下引用了实例“john”。

这就是这个帖子的家伙。 希望您能够毫不费力地理解这些概念。 将有更多关于面向对象概念的帖子。
请继续关注。



原文:

Originally Published on Hashnode

Hi, my name is Adhik 👋. I am a front-end Web developer. You can find me on Twitter.

For as long as object-oriented programming has been around, it is sometimes difficult to get your head around it. In this article and the next coming ones, I will try my best to make these object-oriented concepts easier to understand.

For people who are just starting to learn javascript, may or may not be familiar with ES6+ syntax. Not to worry. We are going to understand in brief about the version before ES6+ came out so that when you start with the ES6+ syntax, you would understand the terminology effortlessly.

We are going to learn how to create objects with the help of constructor functions.

Are you still here? Let's dive right into it.

An object is a collection of properties and methods. For example, a car has properties such as a distinctive color, a brand, type of seats (hand-stitched or leather),etc. You can start a car and accelerate using the accelerator, you can use brakes to stop the car. These are nothing but methods of a Car.

We can create objects in many well-known ways,

  • Using object literal syntax

  • Using the **Object.create() **method

  • Using constructor functions

  1. Using the object literal syntax 
let audi = {
    model: "A4",
    brand: "Audi",
    color: "Metallic Black"
};

let bmw = {
    model: "Z4",
    brand: "BMW",
    color: "Alpine White"
};

In the above example, we create some variables called audi and bmw and wrap them in curly braces ({}). This is how we create an object using the object literal i.e. using curly braces ({}). model, brand and color are nothing but properties of an object.

  1. Using the Object.create() method 
let person = {
    firstName: "John",
    lastName: "Doe",
    age: 42
};

const newPerson = Object.create(person);
newPerson.firstName = "Jane";
newPerson.age = 32;

console.log(newPerson);

The Object.create() method creates a new object, using an existing object as the prototype of the newly created object. An Object inherits properties and methods from the prototype. In simple terms, the object "newPerson" has inherited the properties firstName, lastName and age of the "person" object, because we used the reference of the "person" object while creating the **"newPerson" **object.
We will discuss about the prototype in detail in the next post.

  1. Using constructor functions

While using the object literal syntax above, we defined two objects having same properties inside them. In that example we were referring to two different brands of a car, but the properties and methods of any car would be the same right? The brand, the model, the color would be different but the property names would be the same.

Imagine, we have to create more than 20 objects for every different car object but they all have the same property names and just their values would be different. In that case, creating the object for every brand would be just repeating and not keeping the code DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself). This is where constructor functions come into picture.

In JavaScript, a constructor gets called when an object is created using the new keyword. The purpose of a constructor is to create a new object and set values for any existing object properties.


function Person(firstName, lastName){

  this.firstName = firstName;
  this.lastName = lastName;

  this.greet = function() {
     console.log(`Hi, this is ${this.firstName} ${lastName}`)
  }

}

// Creating an instance of the Person constructor using the "new" operator
const john = new Person("John", "Doe");
john.greet(); //Expected Output: Hi, this is John Doe.

Okay, so let's break down this complex constructor function. We have a function called Person. If you look closely, you will notice that the starting letter of the function name is capitalized. When we define a constructor function or a class (ES6+ concept, will be discussed in the coming posts), its considered to be a good practice to capitalize the first letter of the constructor or a class.

Here, we created an instance of the constructor Person . The "this" keyword references the object that will be created when the constructor is instantiated. In other words, the this keyword in the above example refers to the object properties defined when the instance "john" was created. That is why, we are able to use the method greet of the constructor function as it refers to the instance "john" in this case.

That's it for this post guys. Hope you were able to understand the concepts effortlessly. There will be few more posts regarding object-oriented concepts.
Stay tuned for that.
item.author: prodesed

item.title: SQL-Quick 技巧 #12 - 可用磁盘空间 | SQL-Quick tip #12 - Available disk space
item.description:
艾伦·西蒙森

发表于这是我一年来积累的一系列快速提示和技巧的一部分，我认为它们对其他人有用。
如果您有类似的简短提示和技巧，请发表评论。

可用磁盘空间

让您的数据库服务器耗尽磁盘空间是一件非常糟糕的事情。 它是那种可以真正搞砸你的一天、一周甚至整个月的炫技。 因此，如果您的磁盘空间不足，您应该进行某种监控以警告您。 您可以购买一些昂贵的企业包罗万象的软件来监控一切，或者您可以通过执行计划任务来开始，该任务运行查询以获取有关可用磁盘空间的信息，然后向您发送包含数字的电子邮件。 下面的第一个脚本将显示数据库所在的驱动器上剩余的磁盘空间，第二个脚本将显示当前分配给数据库的磁盘空间以及文件组中有多少可用空间。 

 SELECT DISTINCT dovs . volume_mount_point AS Drive , CONVERT ( INT , dovs . available_bytes / 1048576 . 0 ) AS [ Free Space In MB ] FROM sys . master_files mf CROSS APPLY sys . dm_os_volume_stats ( mf . database_id , mf . FILE_ID ) dovs ORDER BY [ Free Space In MB ] ASC SELECT DS . name [ Filegroup name ], DF . name [ Filename ], SUBSTRING ( DF . physical_name , 1 , 1 ) [ Drive ], DF . physical_name [ Physical name ], size / 128 [ Total size in MB ], size / 128 . 0 - CAST ( FILEPROPERTY ( DF . name , 'SpaceUsed' ) AS int ) / 128 . 0 [ Available space in MB ], CAST ( FILEPROPERTY ( DF . name , 'SpaceUsed' ) AS int ) / 128 . 0 [ Actual space used in MB ], ( CAST ( FILEPROPERTY ( DF . name , 'SpaceUsed' ) AS int ) / 128 . 0 ) / ( size / 128 ) * 100 . [ % Space used ] FROM sys . database_files DF LEFT OUTER JOIN sys . data_spaces DS ON DF . data_space_id = DS . data_space_id ORDER BY DF . Name ASC ;

戈兰·科特杰—— 弗兰克·帕肖 - Dec 21 '21 21 年 12 月 21 日



原文:

Allan Simonsen

Posted on May 10

This is part of a series of quick tips and tricks I have accumulated over the year, that I think can be useful for others.
If you have similar short tips and tricks please leave a comment.

Available disk space

Having your database server running out of disk space is a very bad thing. Its the kind of showstopper that can really mess up your day, week or maybe the entire month. So you should have some kind of monitoring in place to warn you if your disk is running low on space. You can buy some expensive enterprise all-inclusive software the monitors everything, or you start small by implementing scheduled task that runs a query to fetch information about the available disk space and then send you an email with the numbers. The first script below will show how much disk space is left on the drive where your database is located and the second script will show how much disk space is currently allocated to the database and how much free space is available in the filegroup.

SELECT DISTINCT dovs.volume_mount_point AS Drive, CONVERT(INT,dovs.available_bytes/1048576.0) AS [Free Space In MB] FROM sys.master_files mf CROSS APPLY sys.dm_os_volume_stats(mf.database_id, mf.FILE_ID) dovs ORDER BY [Free Space In MB] ASC SELECT DS.name [Filegroup name], DF.name [Filename], SUBSTRING(DF.physical_name, 1, 1) [Drive], DF.physical_name [Physical name], size/128 [Total size in MB], size/128.0 - CAST(FILEPROPERTY(DF.name, 'SpaceUsed') AS int)/128.0 [Available space in MB], CAST(FILEPROPERTY(DF.name, 'SpaceUsed') AS int)/128.0 [Actual space used in MB], (CAST(FILEPROPERTY(DF.name, 'SpaceUsed') AS int)/128.0)/(size/128)*100. [% Space used] FROM sys.database_files DF LEFT OUTER JOIN sys.data_spaces DS ON DF.data_space_id = DS.data_space_id ORDER BY DF.Name ASC;

Goran Kortjie - Dec 23 '21

Josue Luzardo Gebrim - Dec 22 '21

Franck Pachot - Dec 21 '21
item.author: coderallan

item.title: 如何在 React 中上传和预览图像 [详细课程] | How to Upload and Preview Image in React [DETAIL COURSE]
item.description:
如需更多免费和详细的课程，请访问 HTTPS://CODECOURSES.SITE

目录

为了帮助人们学习，所有的课程都是免费和详细的。 出于这个原因，Code Courses 认为您不需要在那里购买任何课程。 希望在学习完 Code Courses 上的内容后，您会找到您梦寐以求的工作，并构建您想要的任何应用程序。

完成本课程后，最终输出将如下所示：

如果你想找到完整的源代码，可以参考这个Github 链接。

我们可以构建上传和预览图像应用程序，并了解如何应用 React 创建一个真实的项目。 我们可以将这个项目包含在我们的个人资料中。 这将有助于我们找到一份软件工程师的工作。 除此之外，我们可以使用从本课程中获得的技能来构建其他相关的应用程序。

正如我们从上图中看到的那样，我们将有上传部分。 如果用户单击该部分，则用户可以从他们的设备中选择图像。 选择图像后，将显示所选图像。 因此，用户可以预览选择的图像。 另一方面，要更改所选图像，用户只需单击所选图像，即可从设备中选择另一个图像。

完成本课程后，我们可以了解如何应用 React 来构建现实生活中的项目。

软件

  • 安装 Node.js。
  • 基本的编程技能。
  • 基本的 HTML、CSS、JS、React 技能。

事实上，我们有几种方法可以创建一个新的 React 项目，例如从 CDN 链接导入 React 库或使用现有的样板/模板。 在这种情况下，我们将使用Create React App创建我们的 React 项目

它设置您的开发环境，以便您可以使用最新的 JavaScript 功能，提供良好的开发人员体验，并优化您的生产应用程序。

创建上传和预览图像应用程序

在这种情况下，要创建我们的上传和预览图像应用程序项目，我们需要按照以下步骤操作：

  • 第 1 步：我们需要在我们的机器上安装Node >= 14.0.0 和 npm >= 5.6 。 如果我们还没有安装 Node.js，请点击上面的链接并按照它的文档进行操作。

  • 第 2 步：为了确保我们已经在我们的计算机上安装了 Node.js。 因此，请打开您的终端/cmd/power shell 并运行以下语句。

结果应该是这样的v16.10.0 。

不过，根据您安装的版本，它在您的计算机上可能会有所不同。

  • 第 3 步：在我们的计算机上安装 Node.js 之后。 在我们的终端上，我们需要使用以下语句。
 npm install - g create - react - app
 create - react - app your - app - name

另外，我们需要将your-app-name替换为我们应用的真实名称。 在这种情况下，我们要构建一个上传和预览图像的应用程序。 因此，我们会将your-app-name替换为upload-preview-image-react 。 总之，现在我们的最终语句应该如下所示：

create - react - app upload - preview - image - react
  • 第4步：否则，我们需要等到该过程完成。 之后，我们的结果应该如下所示：

  • 第 5 步：现在我们可以尝试运行我们的应用程序。 在同一终端上，请cd到您的项目文件夹。
 cd upload - preview - image - react
  • 第 6 步：然后，请运行以下语句来启动我们的 React 项目。

我们的结果应该是这样的

一般来说，我们将讨论如何构建我们的项目。 在其他一些教程中，我们可能会看到这些教程会告诉您将每个组件存储在src文件夹中，或者在这些文件中开发所有内容，而不关心一些最佳实践和原则。 例如，我们不想违反 DRY 原则，DRY 代表don't repeat yourself 。 在大多数情况下，这意味着我们应该避免业务逻辑中的重复。 因此，为避免这种情况，我们创建了一些通用文件和文件夹。 因此，我们可以在不同的地方重复使用它们。 考虑到这个目的，这样做有助于我们提高代码的可读性、可维护性和可扩展性。

项目结构

在本节中，我们将讨论如何构建我们的项目。

  • 步骤 1：首先，请在src文件夹中创建一个名为components的文件夹。

例如登录组件、注册组件、主页组件等。

  • 第2步：此外，我们需要删除本课程中一些未使用的文件。 它们是App.css 、 App.test.js 、 logo.svg 、 reportWebVitals.js 、 setupTests.js 。

  • 第 4 步：在这种情况下，我们在App.js中导入logo.svg文件。 因此，我们需要将其从App.js文件中删除。
 import React from " react " ; const App = () => { return < React . Fragment > Hello , Upload and Preview Image App < /React.Fragment> ; }; export default App ;

在App.js文件中，我们删除了所有依赖项和当前的 JSX 元素。 之后，我们返回了一个 React 片段。 在该片段中，我们展示了Hello, Counter App 。

  • 第 5 步：实际上，我们是在index.js文件中导入reportWebVitals 。 但是，我们在第 3 步中删除reportWebVitals 。因此，我们需要将其从index.js文件中删除。
 import React from " react " ; import ReactDOM from " react-dom/client " ; import " ./index.css " ; import App from " ./App " ; const root = ReactDOM . createRoot ( document . getElementById ( " root " )); root . render ( < App /> );

现在我们可以回到浏览器，UI 应该是这样的。

设置 CSS

事实上，我们需要让我们的 UI 有吸引力。 因此，在本节中，我们将为我们的应用程序设置 CSS。 因此，我们不需要关心样式，因为 CSS 是预先准备好的。

在index.js文件中，我们正在导入index.css文件。 该文件包含应用程序的所有 CSS。 我们有不同的方法来为 React 应用程序设置样式，我们可以使用styled-components库，也可以使用TailwindCSS库，等等。

因此，我们将在index.css文件中写入所有 CSS。

请将index.css文件的当前内容替换为以下内容：

 body { margin : 0 ; font - family : - apple - system , BlinkMacSystemFont , " Segoe UI " , " Roboto " , " Oxygen " , " Ubuntu " , " Cantarell " , " Fira Sans " , " Droid Sans " , " Helvetica Neue " , sans - serif ; - webkit - font - smoothing : antialiased ; - moz - osx - font - smoothing : grayscale ; /* background-color: #112b3c; */ } code { font - family : source - code - pro , Menlo , Monaco , Consolas , " Courier New " , monospace ; } * { padding : 0 ; margin : 0 ; box - sizing : border - box ; } input { outline : none ; border : none ; } :: - webkit - scrollbar { display : none ; } . upload__wrapper { align - items : center ; display : flex ; justify - content : center ; min - height : 100 vh ; min - width : 100 vw ; } . upload__container { display : flex ; flex - direction : column ; } . upload__placeholder { align - items : center ; border - radius : 0.25 rem ; border : 1 px dashed # fff ; color : # fff ; display : flex ; font - size : 1.25 rem ; font - weight : 600 ; justify - content : center ; margin - bottom : 1 rem ; max - height : 15 rem ; max - width : 15 rem ; min - height : 15 rem ; min - width : 15 rem ; padding : 5 rem ; } . upload__placeholder : hover { cursor : pointer ; opacity : 0.8 ; } . upload__content { border - radius : 0.25 rem ; height : 15 rem ; margin - bottom : 1 rem ; object - fit : cover ; width : 15 rem ; } . upload__content : hover { cursor : pointer ; opacity : 0.8 ; }

构建上传组件

如上所述，我们将有上传部分。 如果用户单击该部分，则用户可以从他们的设备中选择图像。 选择图像后，将显示所选图像。 因此，用户可以预览选择的图像。 另一方面，要更改所选图像，用户只需单击所选图像，即可从设备中选择另一个图像。

要开发上传组件，请按照以下步骤操作：

  • 第 1 步：请在components文件夹中创建Upload.js文件。

用户可以点击上传部分来选择他们想要上传的图像。 另一方面，在上传图片后，如果用户想要更改所选图片，他们可以点击该图片，然后他们可以选择另一张图片。 对于每个动作，组件都需要根据所选图像进行渲染。 出于这个原因，我们需要将选中的对象存储在一个状态中。

  • 第 2 步：在Upload.js文件中，我们从react导入useState 。
 import { useState } from ' react ' ;
  • 第 3 步：在Upload.js文件中，我们定义了一个名为Upload的功能组件，该组件将临时渲染一个空的 React 片段。
 import { useState } from ' react ' ; const Upload = () => { return <>< /> ; }; export default Upload ;
  • 第 4 步：为了将所选图像存储为状态，我们定义了一个称为avatar的状态。
 ... const Upload = () => { const [ avatar , setAvatar ] = useState ( null ); }; ...

默认情况下，用户尚未选择任何图像。 因此， avatar状态的默认值为null 。

  • 第五步：在Upload.js文件中，更新return语句如下：
 import { useState } from " react " ; const Upload = () => { const [ avatar , setAvatar ] = useState ( null ); return ( < div className = " upload__wrapper " > < div className = " upload__container " > { ! avatar && ( < div className = " upload__placeholder " > < span > Upload < /span > < /div > )} { avatar && ( < img className = " upload__content " src = { avatar } alt = " img " /> )} < input hidden type = " file " /> < /div > < /div > ); }; export default Upload ;

如果我们运行代码，UI 将是这样的：

在这种情况下，我们在 React 中使用了conditional rendering 。 这意味着如果avatar状态为空，我们将显示上传部分，如上图所示。 另一方面，如果avatar状态不为空，我们将显示选中的图像。

这里有一件特别值得我们注意的事情。 我们写

< input hidden type = " file " />

要从我们的设备中选择图像，我们需要有input元素，它的类型是file 。 在这种情况下，我们不想在屏幕上显示输入元素。 因此，我们为该输入元素使用了hidden属性。 但是，问题是，如果我们不想显示该输入元素，我们如何让用户从他们的设备中选择图像。 这个想法是，如果用户点击上传部分，我们想要触发输入元素的功能。

为此，我们需要使用 React ref（例如，ref 名称可以是filepickerRef ）。 我们将定义一个 ref，然后将该 ref 与输入元素相关联。 之后，我们将处理上传部分的onClick事件（其className为upload_placeholder的div元素）。 每当我们点击上传部分时，我们都会调用：

filepickerRef . current . click ();

另一方面，根据业务需求，用户选择一张图片后，可以通过选择另一张图片来改变选中的图片。 因此，我们还需要通过调用与上面相同的方法来处理其className为upload__content的div id的onClick事件：

filepickerRef . current . click ();
  • 第 6 步：如上所述，我们需要定义一个名为filepickerRef的 ref，在此之前，在Upload.js文件中，我们需要从 'react' 导入useRef 。 但是，在上述步骤中，我们还从 'react' 导入useState 。 因此，我们可以将它们组合成一条语句，如下所示：
 import { useState , useRef } from ' react ' ;
  • 第 7 步：在Upload.js文件中，我们需要定义filepickerRef 。
 import { useRef , useState } from " react " ; const Upload = () => { const [ avatar , setAvatar ] = useState ( null ); const filepickerRef = useRef (); ... }; export default Upload ;
  • 第 8 步：在Upload.js文件中，我们需要将filepickerRef与输入元素相关联。
 ... const Upload = () => { ... return ( ... < input hidden ref = { filepickerRef } type = " file " /> ... ); ... }; ...
  • 第九步：在Upload.js文件中，我们需要处理两个 div 元素（ className为upload__placeholder的 div 元素和className为upload__content的 div 元素）的onClick事件。 请更新return语句如下：
 ... const Upload = () => { return ( < div className = " upload__wrapper " > < div className = " upload__container " > { ! avatar && ( < div className = " upload__placeholder " onClick = {() => filepickerRef . current . click ()} > < span > Upload < /span > < /div > )} { avatar && ( < img className = " upload__content " src = { avatar } alt = " img " onClick = {() => filepickerRef . current . click ()} / > )} < input hidden ref = { filepickerRef } type = " file " /> < /div > < /div > ); }; ...

现在，如果我们运行代码，然后单击上传部分，将显示一个弹出窗口，我们可以从我们的设备中选择一个图像。 请参阅下图了解更多信息。

但是，当我们选择了一张图片后，上传部分不会被隐藏，而是会显示选择的图片。 这背后的原因是我们在从我们的设备中选择了一个图像后，还没有设置avatar状态。

为此，我们需要处理输入元素的onChange 。

  • 第 10 步：在Upload.js文件中，我们处理输入元素的onChange事件。
 ... const Upload = () => { ... return ( ... < input hidden onChange = { uploadAvatar } ref = { filepickerRef } type = " file " /> ... ); ... }; ...

根据上面的代码片段，当我们从我们的设备中选择了一张图片后， uploadAvatar函数将被执行。 我们还没有定义那个函数。 我们将在下面的步骤中做到这一点。

  • 第 11 步：在Upload.js文件中，我们定义uploadAvatar函数。
 ... const Upload = () => { const uploadAvatar = ( e ) => { const reader = new FileReader (); if ( e . target . files [ 0 ]) { reader . readAsDataURL ( e . target . files [ 0 ]); } reader . onload = ( readerEvent ) => { setAvatar ( readerEvent . target . result ); }; }; ... }; ...

我们使用FileReader对象来读取文件内容。

如果选择的文件存在，我们将调用reader.readAsDataURL(e.target.files[0]);

我们还定义了read.onload函数来获取文件内容并更新avatar状态。

在这一部分中，我们将把我们的项目推送到 Github。 为开源社区做出贡献，管理您的 Git 存储库，等等。

要将我们的项目推送到 Github，我们需要按照以下步骤操作：

  • 第 1 步：在根目录中创建.gitignore文件，内容如下。

我们不想将node_modules和.env文件夹推送到 Github。

  • 第 2 步：转到此链接，然后使用您创建的帐户登录 Github。

  • 第 3 步：我们登录到 Github 后，请转到此链接创建一个新的存储库。

  • 第 4 步：我们需要输入存储库名称，然后单击“创建存储库”按钮。

  • 步骤 5：我们需要打开终端， cd到项目文件夹，并按照 Github 上的指南进行操作

  • 第 1 步：请转到此链接并单击Login按钮。

  • 第 2 步：请使用您的 Github 帐户登录。

  • 第三步：登录Vercel平台成功后，我们会在这个页面

恭喜！ 我们已经使用 React 完成了上传和预览图片的应用。 总之，我们已经了解了本课程的目的，并创建并构建了该项目。 之后，我们还开发了 Upload 组件并将应用程序部署到 Vercel。 非常感谢您参加本课程，您可以在Code Courses上找到许多课程。



原文:

FOR MORE FREE AND DETAIL COURSES, PLEASE GO TO HTTPS://CODECOURSES.SITE

Table of Contents

1. About Code Courses

Code Courses is a website where people learn about coding and different technologies/frameworks/libraries. For the purpose of helping people learn, all of the courses are FREE and DETAIL. For this reason, Code Courses believe that you do not need to buy any courses out there. Hopefully, after following the content on Code Courses, you will find your dream jobs, and build any applications that you want.

2. Live Demo

After we finish this course, the final output will be like this:

If you want to find the full source code, you can refer to this Github link.

3. Takeaway Skills

We can build the upload and preview image application and understand how to apply React to create a real-life project. We can include this project in our profiles. It would help us a lot in finding a software engineer job. Aside from that, we can build other related applications with the skills we will get from this course.

4. Course Overview

As we see from the above image, we will have the upload section. If the users click on that section, the users can select an image from their devices. After selecting an image, the selected image will be shown. Therefore, the users can preview the selected image. On the other hand, to change the selected image, the users just need to click on the selected image and they can choose another image from their devices.

After finishing this course, we can understand how to apply React to build a real-life project.

5. Prerequisites

5.1. Softwares

  • Install NodeJS.
  • An IDE or a text editor (VSCode, Intellij, Webstorm, etc).

5.2 Technical Skills

  • Basic programming skills.
  • Basic HTML, CSS, JS, React skills.

6. Creating the React Project

In fact, we have several ways to create a new React project such as importing the React library from CDN links or using existing boilerplates/templates out there. In this case, we will create our React project by using the Create React App

Create React App is a comfortable environment for learning React and is the best way to start building a new single-page application in React. It sets up your development environment so that you can use the latest JavaScript features, provides a nice developer experience, and optimizes your app for production.

6.1. Create Upload and Preview Image App

In this situation, to create our upload and preview image app project, we need to follow the below steps:

  • Step 1: We'll need to have Node >= 14.0.0 and npm >= 5.6 on our machine. In case, If we have not installed Node.js, please click on the above link and follow its documentation.

  • Step 2: In order to make sure that we have installed Node.js on our computer. Hence, please open your terminal/cmd/power shell and run the following statement.

The result should be like this v16.10.0.

16.10.0 is the Node.js version on my computer. Nevertheless, it may be different on your computer, depending on which version you have installed.

  • Step 3: After we have installed Node.js on our computer. On our terminal, we need to use the below statements. 
npm install -g create-react-app

create-react-app your-app-name

In addition, we need to replace your-app-name with the real name of our application. In this case, we want to build an upload and preview image app. For this reason, we will replace your-app-name with upload-preview-image-react. In conclusion, now our final statement should look like this:

create-react-app upload-preview-image-react
  • Step 4: Otherwise, we need to wait until the process is finished. After that, our result should look like this:

  • Step 5: Now we can try to run our application. On the same terminal, please cd to your project folder. 
cd upload-preview-image-react
  • Step 6: Following that, please run the below statement to start our React project.

Our result should look like this

7. Structuring the Project

In general, we will talk about how we structure our project. In some other tutorials, we may see that those tutorials will tell you to store every component in the src folder or develop everything in those files without caring about some best practices and principles. For example, we don't want to violate the DRY principle, DRY stands for don't repeat yourself. For the most part, It means that we should avoid duplication in the business logic. Hence, to avoid that, we create some common files and folders. Therefore, we can reuse them in different places. With this purpose in mind, doing that helps us increase the readability, maintainability, and scalability of our code.

7.1. Project Structure

In this section, we talk about how to structure our project.

  • Step 1: Firstly, please create a folder which is called components inside the src folder.

The components folder will contain all of the components in our application. For example, the login component, the register component, the home component, and so on.

  • Step 2: Moreover, we need to remove some unused files in this course. They are the App.css, App.test.js, logo.svg, reportWebVitals.js, setupTests.js.

  • Step 4: In this situation, we are importing the logo.svg file in the App.js. For that reason, we need to remove it from the App.js file. 
import React from "react"; const App = () => { return <React.Fragment>Hello, Upload and Preview Image App</React.Fragment>;
}; export default App;

In the App.js file, we removed all of the dependencies and the current JSX elements. After that, we returned a React fragment. Inside that fragment, we showed Hello, Counter App.

  • Step 5: In fact, We are importing reportWebVitals in the index.js file. However, we removed reportWebVitals in step 3. Therefore, we need to remove it from the index.js file. 
import React from "react";
import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client";
import "./index.css";
import App from "./App"; const root = ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root"));
root.render(<App />);

Now we can get back to our browser and the UI should be like this.

The full source code of the index.js file will be like this:

import React from "react";
import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client";
import "./index.css";
import App from "./App"; const root = ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root"));
root.render(<App />);

The full source code of the App.js file will be like this:

import React from "react"; const App = () => { return <React.Fragment>Hello, Upload and Preview Image App</React.Fragment>;
}; export default App;

7.2. Setting Up CSS

In fact, we need to make our UI attractive. Therefore, in this section, we will set up CSS for our application. Hence, we do not need to care about styling because CSS was pre-prepared.

In the index.js file, we are importing the index.css file. This file contains all CSS for the application. We have different ways to set up styling for a React application, we can use the styled-components library, or we can use the TailwindCSS library, and so on.

Therefore, we will write all CSS in the index.css file.

Please replace the current content of the index.css file with the following content:

body { margin: 0; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", "Roboto", "Oxygen", "Ubuntu", "Cantarell", "Fira Sans", "Droid Sans", "Helvetica Neue", sans-serif; -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; /* background-color: #112b3c; */
} code { font-family: source-code-pro, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, "Courier New", monospace;
} * { padding: 0; margin: 0; box-sizing: border-box;
} input { outline: none; border: none;
} ::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none;
} .upload__wrapper { align-items: center; display: flex; justify-content: center; min-height: 100vh; min-width: 100vw;
} .upload__container { display: flex; flex-direction: column;
} .upload__placeholder { align-items: center; border-radius: 0.25rem; border: 1px dashed #fff; color: #fff; display: flex; font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 600; justify-content: center; margin-bottom: 1rem; max-height: 15rem; max-width: 15rem; min-height: 15rem; min-width: 15rem; padding: 5rem;
} .upload__placeholder:hover { cursor: pointer; opacity: 0.8;
} .upload__content { border-radius: 0.25rem; height: 15rem; margin-bottom: 1rem; object-fit: cover; width: 15rem;
} .upload__content:hover { cursor: pointer; opacity: 0.8;
}

7.3. Build the Upload Component

As mentioned above, we will have the upload section. If the users click on that section, the users can select an image from their devices. After selecting an image, the selected image will be shown. Therefore, the users can preview the selected image. On the other hand, to change the selected image, the users just need to click on the selected image and they can choose another image from their devices.

To develop the upload component, please follow the below steps:

  • Step 1: Please create the Upload.js file inside the components folder.

The users can click on the upload section to choose the image that they want to upload. On the other hand, after uploading an image, if the users want to change the selected image, they can click on that image and they can choose another image. For each action, the component needs to be rendered based on the selected image. For this reason, we need to store the selected in a state.

  • Step 2: In the Upload.js file, we import useState from react. 
import { useState } from 'react';
  • Step 3: In the Upload.js file, we define a functional component which is called Upload and that component will render an empty React fragment temporarily. 
import { useState } from 'react'; const Upload = () => { return <></>;
}; export default Upload;
  • Step 4: To store the selected image as a state, we define a state which is called avatar. 
...
const Upload = () => { const [avatar, setAvatar] = useState(null);
};
...

By default, the users have not selected any image, yet. Hence, the default value of the avatar state will be null.

  • Step 5: In the Upload.js file, please update the return statement as follows: 
import { useState } from "react"; const Upload = () => { const [avatar, setAvatar] = useState(null); return ( <div className="upload__wrapper"> <div className="upload__container"> {!avatar && ( <div className="upload__placeholder" > <span>Upload</span>
 </div>
 )} {avatar && ( <img className="upload__content" src={avatar} alt="img" /> )} <input hidden type="file" /> </div>
 </div>
 );
}; export default Upload;

If we run the code, the UI will be like this:

In this case, we used conditional rendering in React. It means that if the avatar state is null, we will show the upload section as we can see from the above image. On the other hand, if the avatar state is not null, we will display the selected image.

There is a special thing that we should notice here. We wrote

<input hidden type="file" />

To select an image from our devices, we need to have the input element and its type is file. In this case, we do not want to display the input element on the screen. Hence, we used the hidden property for that input element. However, the question is that if we do not want to display that input element, how can we let the users choose an image from their devices. The idea is that if the users click on the upload section, we want to trigger the functionality of the input element.

To achieve that, we need to use React ref (for example, the ref name can be filepickerRef). We will define a ref and then associate that ref with the input element. After that, we will handle the onClick event for the upload section (the div element that its className is upload_placeholder). Whenever we click on the upload section, we will call:

filepickerRef.current.click();

On the other hand, according to the business requirements, after the users have selected an image, they can change the selected image by selecting another image. Therefore, we also need to handle the onClick event for the div id that its className is upload__content by calling the same thing as above:

filepickerRef.current.click();
  • Step 6: As mentioned above, we need to define a ref which is called filepickerRef, before doing that, in the Upload.js file, we need to import useRef from 'react'. However, in the above step, we also imported useState from 'react'. Therefore, we can combine them into a single statement as follows: 
import { useState, useRef } from 'react';
  • Step 7: In the Upload.js file, we need to define the filepickerRef. 
import { useRef, useState } from "react"; const Upload = () => { const [avatar, setAvatar] = useState(null); const filepickerRef = useRef(); ...
}; export default Upload;
  • Step 8: In the Upload.js file, we need to associate the filepickerRef with the input element. 
...
const Upload = () => { ... return ( ... <input hidden ref={filepickerRef} type="file" /> ... ); ...
};
...
  • Step 9: In the Upload.js file, we need to handle the onClick event for two div elements (the div element that its className is upload__placeholder and the div element that its className is upload__content). Please update the return statement as follows: 
...
const Upload = () => { return ( <div className="upload__wrapper"> <div className="upload__container"> {!avatar && ( <div className="upload__placeholder" onClick={() => filepickerRef.current.click()} > <span>Upload</span>
 </div>
 )} {avatar && ( <img className="upload__content" src={avatar} alt="img" onClick={() => filepickerRef.current.click()} />
 )} <input hidden ref={filepickerRef} type="file" /> </div>
 </div>
 );
};
...

Now if we run the code and then we click on the upload section, a popup will be displayed, and we can select an image from our devices. Please refer to the below image for more information.

However, after we have selected an image, the upload section will not be hidden, and the selected image will be displayed. The reason behind this is that we have not set the avatar state after we have selected an image from our devices.

To do that, we need to handle the onChange for the input element.

  • Step 10: In the Upload.js file, we handle the onChange event for the input element. 
...
const Upload = () => { ... return ( ... <input hidden onChange={uploadAvatar} ref={filepickerRef} type="file" /> ... ); ...
};
...

According to the above code snippet, after we have selected an image from our devices, the uploadAvatar function will be executed. We have not defined that function, yet. We will do that in the below step.

  • Step 11: In the Upload.js file, we define the uploadAvatar function. 
...
const Upload = () => { const uploadAvatar = (e) => { const reader = new FileReader(); if (e.target.files[0]) { reader.readAsDataURL(e.target.files[0]); } reader.onload = (readerEvent) => { setAvatar(readerEvent.target.result); }; }; ...
};
...

The uploadAvatar function will accept React synthetic event as its parameter. We used the FileReader object to read the file content.

If the selected file exists, we will call reader.readAsDataURL(e.target.files[0]);

We also defined the read.onload function to get the file content and update the avatar state.

8. Pushing the Project to Github

In this part, we will push our project to Github. GitHub is where over 73 million developers shape the future of software, together. Contribute to the open-source community, manage your Git repositories, and so on.

To push our project to Github, we need to follow the below steps:

  • Step 1: Create the .gitignore file in your root directory with the following content.

Vercel combines the best developer experience with an obsessive focus on end-user performance.
